text:
"Les cieux se réjouissent !" Un rayon de joie spontanée perce les murs soigneusement entretenus de l'Archicurée.

"Vous devez l'amener ici, il faut que je le voie," elle marque une pause, puis rit d'elle-même. "Vous voyez ce que je veux dire. Venez, je vais demander aux Chevaliers de renoncer à leur rigidité coutumière."

Elle coupe sa partie de la liaison comm avec une rapidité inhabituelle, vous laissant avec une image d'attente statique fournie par l'Église : une silhouette humaine descendant sur Gilead dans un rayon de lumière.

lke_endingStartA:"Ce n'est pas Mazalot, hein."
lke_endingStartB:"Comment allez-vous, $global.jethroName ?"

text:
Il sourit, reconnaissant l'absurdité de votre remarque.

"Mazalot n'était pas si terrible. Il y avait là-bas une sorte de liberté introuvable sur Gilead, même si le danger était grand également."

Il se retourne vers la vue, "Le vert m'a manqué."

Il sourit poliment. "Anxieux, je l'avoue. Je crois que je serai puni par les Chevaliers, bien que la Révérende Archicurée puisse tenter de me protéger. Je lui demanderai de ne pas le faire. Un serment est un serment."

"Je suppose qu'il y aura beaucoup de contemplation silencieuse et de récurage de sols." Il a l'air songeur, "Ils aiment bien ce genre de choses. Cela ne me dérange pas." Il se retourne face à Gilead.

Vous êtes accueilli sur l'aire d'atterrissage par un Chevalier luddic solennel accompagné d'un Chevalier Initié. Le froncement de sourcils du Chevalier s'accentue visiblement lorsqu'il aperçoit Bornanew. 

Le Chevalier Ailanthus vous conduit au bureau de l'Archicurée Jaspis dans un silence par ailleurs réprobateur. Bornanew prend ce traitement avec sérénité, profitant des jardins et de l'air frais.

script:
BeginConversation jaspis
ShowSecondPerson bornanew
AdjustRepPerson jaspis FRIENDLY 15
text:
Lorsque vous entrez dans le bureau de Jaspis, le Chevalier Ailanthus inspire pour vous annoncer - et l'Archicurée l'interrompt. 

"Jethro, te voilà !" dit-elle, "La Providence pourvoit !" Le Chevalier Ailanthus laisse échapper une toux, et Jaspis répond en les congédiant d'un geste.

"Tout autant, je dois l'attester, grâce aux efforts du bon capitaine dont j'entends aussi les pas," dit-elle en souriant, "Car n'oublions pas que le Chemin du juste ne se parcourt pas tout seul, n'est-ce pas ?"

Bornanew prend la petite main fragile de Jaspis dans la sienne. "Oui, bien sûr. Je vous en prie, Mère Sophronia, vous ne devez pas trop vous agiter. Ce n'est que moi, et je vous ai causé beaucoup de tracas."

"Oui, en effet," dit-elle en repoussant Bornanew. "Et pour cela, vous avez déjà décidé de vous soumettre à quelque démonstration de pénitence insensée, je n'en doute pas. Une habitude déplacée apprise des Chevaliers."

"Capitaine," elle se tourne vers vous. "Vous devez vous lasser de tout cela, aussi serai-je brève : vous avez ma plus profonde gratitude. Mon secrétaire veillera à ce que vous receviez votre paiement."

Elle se dirige vers la porte, "Degory, venez !"

Tandis que vous partez avec Degory, vous entendez Jaspis s'adresser à Bornanew.

"L'équilibre des pouvoirs a changé ; tant moi que l'avenir de la Foi avons besoin de vous. Mais..."

"Pourquoi 'Jeff' ?" demande-t-elle.

Vous n'entendez pas sa réponse.

options: BFIN_start2b:"A call from Jeff? Of course, put him through."

text:
"Captain $playerName. I do not forget your assistance." He makes a crooked smile.

The link is poor-quality; his voice is hollowed, lacking the warmth you remember.

"I would ask, beg if I must, for your service once more."

"What I ask, it is a part of the penance I must serve." He raises a hand, as if to stop your objections, "I do also believe it will also serve a spiritual and intrinsic good." 

"And," he clears his throat, "for your assistance, a substantial reward of credits has been authorized by the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis."

"The service..." He releases a breath. "An object considered holy by some in the mother Church has appeared in the possession of a wealthy collector."
options:
BFIN_start3a:"There's been a theft?"
BFIN_start3b:"Did this collector not make a large enough tithe?"

text:
"No," he says, looking up. "That would have been simple. This object, this 'relic', is by all appearances still untouched on Tartessus, in the archives of the Cathedral of Holy Exodus."

"The curates of the archive were in some panic at word that they had lost a holy relic. They checked very, very carefully. I too have personally confirmed that it remains in their keeping."
options:
BFIN_start4a:"So it's a fake. No problem."
BFIN_start4b:"And this is embarrassing to the Church?"

text: "Ah," he gives a weary smile. "The collector is quite secular, and has no relationship with the Church."

"But it could be," $global.jethroName says hesitantly. "It raises... questions."

"And those may become a problem. It is a complex situation and potentially dangerous."

"It could be," $global.jethroName says. "This collector has not been silent about his new acquisition."

"Naturally, the Church does not approve. At the same time, mere disapproval is no reason to cause a diplomatic incident, to say nothing of... well, it is a complex situation. And potentially dangerous."

"If you choose to assist me," he says, "then I shall share with you what, ah, 'intel' I have managed to piece together."

He looks almost embarrassed for a moment, then forces himself proceed. "Credits, yes. I have also been instructed to offer a payment of 50,000 credits when - if - you join my investigation, and an additional 50,000 credits upon completion of my quest."

"If it is possible." He sighs, "And that I cannot promise."

options: BFIN_askCredits:"50k down, and how much on completion? You didn't say."

BFIN_askConflicts:"Now that I've agreed, I need to know of any potential factional conflicts."
BFIN_dontConsider:"It sounds like you don't consider this object to be a legitimate holy relic."
BFIN_howPenance:"Just how much penance are they going to make you do?"

text: "Truly?" He sighs, deflated at this revelation. "The arighting of one errant novice cannot be worth that sum."

"My apologies," he says, opening a battered notebook. "I am unused to this sort of, of bookkeeping."

"Yes, 50,000 upfront, and another 50,000 credits upon resolution. It is hoped that the false artifact might be removed from consideration, one way or another."

He gives you a questioning look. "Without causing an interfactional incident, that is."

"Of course," he says, opening a battered notebook. "The Church anticipates expenses. Well," he grimaces, "Reverend Archcurate Jaspis does."

"Yes, 50,000 upfront, and another 50,000 credits upon satisfactory resolution. It is hoped that the false artifact might be removed from consideration, one way or another."

He gives you a questioning look. "Without causing an interfactional incident, that is."

"This is only the beginning, I am certain," he says with resigned humor. "Violation of Knightly vows is no small falsity, and I am uncertain even that I am truly meant to 'solve' this task set before me."

He pauses, then speaks more quietly, "I fear that my failure might be intended to chasten Jaspis. The Reverend Archcurate, I mean." He looks upward, and lets out a sigh. "She has put altogether too much faith in me."

"It is... trying," he says. "I am not, I am sure, meant to 'solve' this quest set before me to my violation of Knightly vows. Remorse, contrition... those I give freely, and wholly."

He speaks more quietly, "I speculate that my failure and disgrace is being arranged to humble the influence of the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis." He shakes his head, "She has put altogether too much faith into me."

"A reasonable concern." Bornanew rubs his chin. "First and without doubt, a notable member of a Persean League gens is involved." 

"The rest are deduction and speculation." He starts counting off fingers, "The Cathedral of Holy Exodus has much to lose if corruption is revealed in their house; I suspect an insider or an infiltrator."

Next finger, "Within the greater Church, there is a loosely organized sect who, ah, back the theological legitimacy of relics. They would doubtless have forceful opinions on the subject of a duplicate relic, and that in secular hands."

Another finger, "The Luddic Path has much to gain from discord within the Church, and they have proximity to... the problem."

Next finger, "Though it is possible that the criminal underworld has found a means of profit here, possibly Kanta's organization. The pirate queen has an especial taste for desecration, though has demonstrated no particular hatred for the Church beyond her habitual misanthropy." He lets his hands fall.

"Beyond that, I grasp for tenuous threads, captain."

"Um. It is so obvious?" Bornanew almost looks guilty. Almost.

"The Reverend Archcurate did say that my feelings on the subject are... not easily veiled."

"This investigation requires discretion." He brings his hands together. "I shall be mindful of my disposition."

Bornanew puts his hands together, and lowers his head for a moment.

"I thank you for that, captain. In the meantime, I will do what research and inquiries I can with the resources I am provided."

"If you wish to assist my quest, then come to Gilead. The Knights have provided me an entry in the comms directory in order to further my penance."

Bornanew puts his hands together, and lowers his head.

"I understand. If you change your mind, come to Gilead. The Knights have provided me an entry in the comms directory in order to further my penance."

Bornanew puts his hands together, and lowers his head.

"I thank you for that, captain. In the meantime, I will do what research and inquiries I can with the resources I am provided."

"If you wish to assist me in my quest, contact me here on Gilead. The Knights have provided me an entry in the comms directory in order to further my penance."

Bornanew puts his hands together, and lowers his head for a moment.

"I understand. If you change your mind, return here, to Gilead. The Knights have provided me an entry in the comms directory in order to further my penance."

text:
Bornanew looks almost surprised at your words. He does not pray, or thank Providence. He simply looks directly at you through the waver of the comm-link.

"Thank you, captain. Against all odds, you've cast a light upon my path toward redemption."

Perhaps apologetic for the moment of earnestness, he quickly adds, "I am prepared to leave when you are ready for me. I shall await word of your shuttle."
Traffic control is mercifully efficient when arranging transport for a Knight of Ludd.

$option = BFFI_onBoardingYarbResp 0
FireBest DialogOptionSelected
text:
Novice Bornanew speaks from the head of the table on which he has placed a paper notebook. He seems disinclined to use the shared workspace of the holo visualization suite; you're not even sure he brought a datapad.

"...collector who now possesses this duplicate relic is a man by the name Menes Yaribay, a minor member of Gens Yaribay of Kazeron," he looks to you for a sign of recognition.

Of course you know the Yaribays. You were given Horus Yaribay's diplomatic credentials to arrange a meeting with High Hegemon Daud.

Horus Yaribay might owe you a favor, depending on how he views the situation.

Of course you know the Yaribays. You were given Horus Yaribay's diplomatic credentials to arrange a meeting with High Hegemon Daud and have, on at least one occasion, used those credentials to call a favor on account of Gens Yaribay's prestige.

Hopefully he didn't find out.
options:

BFFI_onBoardingYarb2noConfess:Explain, but leave out the abuse of credentials part

text:
Of course you know the Yaribays. You were given Horus Yaribay's diplomatic credentials to arrange a meeting with High Hegemon Daud. During that meeting, you handed over the key required for an encrypted channel between Horus Yaribay and Baikal Daud.

Horus Yaribay might owe you a favor, depending on how he views the situation.

You know of Gens Yaribay of Kazeron, one of the three dominant ruling family-corporations. Theirs is most inclined toward rapprochement with the Hegemony, focusing less on heavy military industry and more on interfactional ventures.
options: BFFI_onBoardingYarb2:"Yes, I've heard of the Yaribays."

text:
Bornanew looks concerned as you describe your dealings. "Your conscience, your soul, is between you and- well, I am not your judge or confessor."

"From a practical standpoint, I would advise caution in asking too much of Horus of Gens Yaribay, to his face or behind it. He may know but choose not to act on your transgression."

A brief look of concern crosses Bornanew's face, and he pauses as if composing an awkward question. He puts his hand back down on the table, seeming to dismiss the notion, and responds in a different tone.
notes: # 'course he knows.

text: "This is providential," he says, nodding. "Your previous dealings with Horus Yaribay could open an easier path to resolving the Church's problem."
He nods and proceeds.

"The question, then, is how Horus Yaribay, the nominal gens leader, views Menes Yaribay's recent interest in religious artifacts."

Bornanew flips through his paper book, not really taking the time to read the dense notes therein. He runs a finger across lists of names.

"Not long ago Menes Yaribay was assigned the office of demarch, in essence the executive administrator, of Olinadu in the Kumari Kandam system. It is not a prestigious post. Olinadu is a small mining colony and the system is rife with Luddic Path activity. It is a dangerous, corrupt place."

Bornanew eye's return to you. "This is why I need your help."

options: BFFI_onBoardAskMenes:"You've done your research. Tell me about Menes Yaribay himself."
BFFI_onBoardAskInCharge:"How did a Kazeron aristocrat get put in charge of Olinadu?"
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic:"Tell me more about the relic at the center of this. What is it?"
BFFI_onBoardAskTart4:"Wait, why's the real glove on Tartessus if Ludd removed it on Gilead?"
BFFI_onBoardAskPlan:"What's the plan? Ask Menes nicely for the glove?"
BFFI_onBoardAskChalcedon:"Kumari Kandam? You were on Chalcedon. With the Path."

text:
"Indeed I was." He's steady. "You know of this. There I met Wrestling Sedge of the Luddic Path, and there I left my vows. For a time."

A pause. "You followed my path, and spoke with the man Sedge. He told you where I went from there, Mazalot, where you found me."

"Indeed I was." He's steady as he speaks. "You know of this. There I met Wrestling Sedge of the Luddic Path, and there I left my vows. For a time."

A pause. "And you killed the man Sedge," he says. "The Path will have no love for you there, but I trust that you will be prepared for... that."

"Certain tawdry media outlets in the outer League worlds and beyond report rumor and gossip about affairs, social vendettas, and... how shall I put this... decadent festivities undertaken by Menes. More so in his younger years, at least." Bornanew turns pages in his notebook, not reading, but recalling as he goes through the motions with his hands.

"His enthusiasm for such pursuits has waned from the cycles of his youth, and his interests have shifted. At heart, I suspect Menes is still a man who wishes for all to hear his name, and for all who hear it to be very impressed."

"Unfortunately for his ambition, he is a natural dilettante in all pursuits. He avoids challenge, seeking instead flattery and the appearance of success which may be purchased with credits." Bornanew reports this without obvious outward judgment, as if discussing the results of a gravimetric survey.

"To his gens, Menes has given only scandal and debt. I suspect he was placed in the Olinadu administration to limit his ability to further damage their reputation."

Bornanew smiles grimly now. "It is some irony that he has potentially stumbled into more trouble than ever."

"Gens Yaribay has a controlling interest in the military corporation which exercises the monopoly of violence for the polity of Olinadu. The Pathers are an ever-present threat, so those who don't cooperate... well, that is the stick which compels. The carrot, on the other hand, is a stream of investment credits facilitated by Gens Yaribay brokers."

"The locals cooperate with this arrangement, and agreed to vote Menes Yaribay to an executive position, perhaps understanding that he'd play the role as a figurehead rather than a serious administrator. Olinadu receives investment and safety while, from all accounts, Menes Yaribay simply wants to be liked."

"Compared to the alternatives," says Bornanew, "he is an ideal overlord."

"Yes, the... artifact." Bornanew looks down, clasping his hands together, then taking them apart and setting them deliberately on the table.

"Ludd's right-hand glove," an abashed smile appears.

"A standard work-glove of Domain manufacture. Light vac-suit compatible. The story is that Ludd removed it to touch the ground of Gilead."
options:
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic1a:"And you don't think it's real? The original, I mean."
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic1b:"Seems like a lot to get worked up about."
BFFI_onBoardAskRelic1c:"What's Ludd going to do with two right-hand gloves?"

text:
"It's a real glove," he says, "That much is true."

"It's just a work glove, one of many designs prevalent through the immediate pre- and post-Collapse Persean Sector."
"I think," he begins, "it is a matter of seeking a physical connection with the sacred."
Bornanew laughs. "Yes, exactly."

"My objection is that the glove, even if it is the glove, is not important. What is important is Ludd's message, Ludd's sacrifice; it is the act of a divine moral force touching the soul of humanity with supreme grace."

"What is a glove to the light of Revelation?" The words come out forcefully.

"Even if it were Ludd's glove - and that we can't, and should not, be sure of - would God love you more for possessing it? Touching, looking upon, and worshipping it?"

His brow furrows. "Then some would try to extract DNA from the glove. Clone Ludd, they say, to re-open a connection to God." He shakes his head furiously, "As if you could fool God with the twin of the prophet's body!"

"Or the glove that body might have worn."

"It's the fool's logic of 'magic' of the lowest kind, the heretical notion that humanity might manipulate the Divine to our own ends. Creation turned on its head!"

You've never seen Bornanew like this, his voice like a river; his physical size giving power to his words. Seeing you see this, he realizes it himself.

"I apologize for my outburst, captain," he says, looking down.

"Why should Menes leave you alone with the relic, or at all?" He looks over at you. 

"I am a Knight of Ludd, devoted and taciturn. I shall simply... display the power of my faith."

"Pray forgive me, captain; I do no question your faith." He presses his hands together.

"But Menes Yaribay cannot see into your soul. He will see your uniform, and my robes. That will be enough."

"We attend Menes' party. Be interesting. Be yourself. Get Menes to show off the glove." 

"I know this trade, captain. Have faith." He pauses for a moment, acknowledging some irony in his own words. "It works best if you act natural."
He crosses his arms. "I am not."

Bornanew bows his head to your words. His expression is grim.

Menes has not been discreet. As a celebration of his first cycle as the new demarch of Olinadu, he invited a holodoc team for a puff-piece interview and viewing of his 'collection'. Your tactical team reviews the holos, constructing schematic of the interior of Menes' complex, inferring layout and defenses from this and other publicly available information.

It is estimated that roughly 100 marines will be required to pull off the operation.

Your marines will be equipped with an eclectic mix of equipment to allow them to pose as high-level pirates. They will be secretly infiltrated into Olinadu as, variously, spacer crew on shore-leave, contract technicians, and ne'er-do-wells looking to spend ill-gotten gains somewhere away from heavy law enforcement.

It goes without saying that you shall need to approach Olinadu with your $shipOrFleet's transponder off. Menes' overly charitable outlook on your character and generalized inattention to his official duties should allow you to join his 'party' with little oversight about how you actually got there.

To avert suspicion and ensure optimal timing, you and Bornanew will personally attend Menes' party when the raid is executed.

The raid will commence when two of a series of randomly generated time-slots are passed without receiving a stand-down signal. This signal will be, by all appearances, a routine status query to your $shipOrFleet, and appear to be perfectly routine behaviour for a justifiably neurotic independent $shipOrFleet operator.

Bornanew is watchful throughout the planning process, only occasionally advocating for lower-force protocols when a particularly bloodthirsty marine squad leader expresses more enthusiasm than strictly required for certain tactical details.

bffi_talkHorusPartyOptB:"He bought a sacred Luddic relic for his personal collection."
bffi_talkHorusPartyOptC:"How do you get on with Menes? Not well?"

text:
Horus blinks, and turns his head aside as if he didn't hear you correctly. His eyes snap back to yours through the comm-link.

"Elaborate upon your statement, if you would be so kind."

A harsh note enters his tone, despite ostensibly polite words.

Horus takes this in, looking into the middle distance, processing for a moment. He takes a slow breath.

"You are certain? Do the Luddics know about it?"
options: bffi_talkHorusPartyTheyKnow:"Oh, they know. They hired me to... handle it."

text: "He is family." Horus gives you a flinty look. "State your meaning."

He closes his eyes and rubs his temples. After a moment, a mutter emerges, "Good grief."

He takes several more long breaths.
options: bffi_talkHorusPartyInvite:"Menes invited me to some kind of party to view this relic."

text:
"He did," Horus stares at you.

"It appears as though I am going to have to become involved," he says. "Personally."

"Again." His jaw is clenched. It looks uncomfortable.
options:
bffi_talkHorusPartyEnd:Ne rien dire.
bffi_talkHorusPartyEndA:"Again?"
bffi_talkHorusPartyEndB:"You need to keep better track of your cousin's schemes, huh."

text:
"The past matters of my gens do not oblige discussion with outsiders, $playerSirOrMadam," he says stiffly.

"However. I do appreciate your... consideration in bringing the current matter to my attention."
"Thank-you captain, that is fabulous advice," Horus says in a tone demonstrating very little appreciation for your input.

"I shall make my own way to Olinadu, thank you. Have your people coordinate with my people."

"We'll sort out Menes' indiscretion and set things right with the Church," he says brusquely, then turns away.

"After all I've done for that ungrateful-" 

The comms cut.
Now you have a party to attend - with an uninvited guest of honor.

options: 3:bffi_attendPartyIntro1:Accept Menes Yaribay's invitation and shuttle to his complex

script:
SetEnabled bffi_attendPartyIntro1 false
SetTooltip bffi_attendPartyIntro1 "You must approach Olinadu with your transponder off in order to sneak marines into position."

SetTooltipHighlights 100

text:
Menes' invitation provides you with a flight path and Demarch-level traffic control override codes.

It's probably a violation of Persean League ethical guidelines for you to be using these to attend a personal party, but from what you can overhear of the pilot's comms, the locals have a casual attitude toward the arrangement.

You and Bornanew make an 'undocumented' arrival on Olinadu then transfer to a private shuttle service in order to attend Menes' party.

Your hired pilot gives a shrug as she shows you a flight path blocked out with red DENIED tags - until you apply the key attached to your invitation, which turns out to be a Demarch-level override code.

It's probably a violation of Persean League ethical guidelines for you to be using these to attend a personal party, but the pilot simply smiles and proceeds. This sort of thing isn't unusual under Menes' administration, it appears.

Menes Yaribay's compound is a scrubbed-looking slab of trendy architecture set on a bluff overlooking Olinadu's primary habitation cluster.

A smattering of outbuildings and hangars, attached by fat trunks of conduit and glowing walktubes, sprawl across the less-fashionable view-angles of the compound toward the refineries and holding tanks of the planet's industrial center.

One set of hangar bay doors dutifully open, and your shuttle descends through the glowing atmo-field. Such gadgetry is an ostentatious addition to a personal residence, but it avoids the bother of visitors (and their shuttles) having to undergo pressure-cycling.

An enthusiastic Menes is already approaching your shuttle's access ramp as it sets itself down and signals the all-clear.

Bornanew exchanges a look with you, then rises to disembark.

"Captain $playerName," Menes crosses his chest with one arm, hand on heart, the other held behind, giving a small bow. "Allow me to welcome you to my home."

"And you've brought your fascinating friend!" he exclaims, as if it was a surprise. "This must be...?"
options:
BFFI_attendParty3a:"This is $global.jethroName."
BFFI_attendParty3b:"Jethro Bornanew, a novice of the Knights of Ludd."

text: "Jethro Bornanew," he adds quickly. "Novice Bornanew of the Knights of Ludd, at your service. May this day be blessed by Providence."
"May this day be blessed by Providence," he says.

Bornanew nods, not quite a bow, with his hands held together. Perhaps to avoid Menes' much-ringed hands from grabbing his. You are not so lucky, and must endure an overlong handshake.

Menes is delighted at all of this, eyes flashing with something akin to greed.

Menes leads you past gilded statues posed to guard the portal to the main complex; the forms are evocative of splashes of molten gold frozen by happenstance into sensual humanoid figures.

You endure a rolling barrage of patter from Menes about the expense of renovations, the acquisition of artworks, the exclusivity of access to particular artists and engineering firms who have contributed to his home, his manor, and its collection.

Occasionally a robotic or human servitor appears to offer drinks or nibbles, and you've a growing feeling that Menes has picked up a glass from one only to place it back on the tray of another without ever taking a sip between the two.

A fresh glass in hand, delicate amber liquid fizzing away, Menes leads you into what can only be his prize entertaining space.

The space is at once cavernous and intimate; the sightlines and carved, wrought, and woven details calculated to replicate the appearance of cozy warmth like the den of some thoughtful patron of art, history, and literature.

A double handful of well-dressed remoras mill about, interrupting their conversations to smile and lift a glass as Menes enters. They look to be a collection of industrialists, financiers, distant gens-relatives, and at least one officer from atop the local military detachment who appears a little worse for his drink. Most nod to you and Bornanew, eyes not lingering to the point of impoliteness.

One skinny man in a finely embroidered suit has no such compunction, and rushes to greet your party, eyes agog at the newest additions to Menes' collection.

He nearly trips over himself to speak. "The great Captain $playerName! If half of what Menes has said is true... why, half the Sector must tremble at the appearance of your $shipOrFleet!"

"We'll have to ask Navarch Atreus over there what he thinks of your combat record," the man grins wickedly, "If he hasn't had too many of these, of course; with all thanks to our kind host, a true gentleman and scholar." He lifts his glass approvingly at Menes, and you see his eyes slide toward Bornanew.

"And Jethro Bornanew, I believe? A Knight of Ludd?"
"I don't recall an introduction," Bornanew says, his hands still held piously together, allowing him to deniably reject both drink and the touch of the present company.
options:
bffi_attendPartyHub1Intro4a:"Pleased to meet you...?"
bffi_attendPartyHub1Intro5b:Ne rien dire
bffi_attendPartyHub1Intro5c:"No need to be prickly, $global.jethroName, the man is being friendly."

text:
Bornanew forces out the equivalent of a smile.

"Of course, captain." He turns to the man. "Pray forgive me, sir, my forgotten manners," he says.

Menes laughs heartily.

"Let me introduce Ulmus Pond," he says. "Now he'll claim to be a mere commodities trader," Menes winks as if you are in on a shared joke, "but I daresay he knows everyone of any importance in the Kumari Kandam system. And if you need anything." He lowers his voice, "Anything at all. He's your man."

Pond, grinning widely, makes a brushing-off gesture as if to dismiss Menes' words - but not so assertively as to deny them.

bffi_apHub1optF:"I'm certain this party will be absolutely unforgettable."

Pond laughs a little too loudly. "Of course we've just met, but dear Menes spoke of a Knight from some unseemly background, and it's a tale which can't but seize the imagination, am I wrong?"

Pond laughs too loudly. "How odd! Dear Menes, where did you find such a fantastic character? I, for one, cannot wait to discover what this evening has in store."

Menes slaps a hand on Pond's shoulder, causing him to nearly spill his drink.

"Ulmus, Ulmus! You'll have plenty of time to speak with the good captain later. I did promise a surprise, a private showing for $playerHimOrHer and $playerHisOrHer noble Knight. You might know the piece, my friend," he winks again, this time to Pond.

"Come along, come along!" Menes says, grabbing your arm. Ulmus Pond's eyes follow you, fawning oozingly.

notes: # stop winking dude

text:
Menes leads you and Bornanew to his inner sanctum, flashing a grin as he operates a series of obtrusive biometric checks.

The air cools by a degree, perhaps two, the armored mass of the walls felt as a dullness through your feet. The subdued lighting, drapery, and framing of the space suggest the interior of a bank vault or a shrine, though not to the praise of any Luddic virtue.

Quite the contrary, depending on one's outlook.

On a pedestal of carved wood, in a spot of light like holy revelation, a single glove is upthrust. It's fingers appear frozen in the midst of just starting to curl back toward a relaxed state after giving you a little wave.

"Do you know what this is?" Menes whispers to you. 

"The Right-Hand Glove of Blessed Ludd," Bornanew answers, stepping forward, voice filled with trembling reverence.

"Removed by the Blessed Ludd, whereupon that prophet fell to the sacred Earth of Gilead to kiss that holy ground. Blessings be upon Ludd; Good is the Word; By the Holy Pneuma; By the Divine Light which illuminates the righteous Path through the Garden of Temptation..."
notes: # Jeff spends a story point

text:
Bornanew himself falls to his knees, half-whispering, half-sobbing in prayer, rocking back and forth, his eyes wide and focus vague. 

This is a display you have never seen from $global.jethroName, completely at odds with his usual air of reserve.

Menes steps back with you, looking somehow appalled and delighted at the same time. He stares at Bornanew like a voyeur.
options:
bffi_mpSteal6:Attendre a bit
bffi_mpSteal5:"He's overwhelmed... you must understand, Menes."

text: "Yes, yes," he replies under his breath.

Bornanew keeps at it, absolutely rapt upon his prayer toward the glove, tears and phlegm running down his face. It is, truly, a pathetic and uncomfortably intimate sight.

Menes keeps staring, an open half-smile on his lips.
options: bffi_mpSteal7:"Menes. We should let him be for a moment, don't you think? Menes?"

text: "Hmm? Oh. Oh yes. Of course, captain," he says, still watching.
options: bffi_mpSteal8:Gently pull Menes' away and ask about other items in the collection

text:
It takes a slightly more than polite tug to overcome Menes' inertia, but finally he's dislodged from Bornanew's spectacle toward another of the handful of chambers making up this 'inner sanctum'.

Menes keeps glancing back toward Bornanew as he explains the story of one or another object: A captain's uniform of the Fourteenth Battlegroup, sword and all, from the period of the Cold Passage; a bottle of spirits from some core Domain star system near Old Earth; a pistol once owned by the Warlord Leonis; the crystalline xenocoral of a species extinct for half a billion years collected by the Exploraria.
options:
bffi_mpSteal9a:"Astounding. Makes one think how we would handle the Cold Passage. Menes?"
bffi_mpSteal9b:"Incredible. What would it take to persuade you to hold a tasting? Menes?"
bffi_mpSteal9c:"Fascinating. Do you think Leonis shot anyone with this, Menes?"
bffi_mpSteal9d:"Intriguing. I wonder if I might find more such xenolife on the fringe. Menes?"

text: "Huh? Oh yes, naturally I've ruminated often upon how my own character might be tested by such hardship. I admit, my life has been one of relative ease, but I like to think that I identify with certain qualities of those early heroic figures whose steadfastness effected the..."

"Ah? Oh yes, I cannot deny the temptation to open it for some appropriate event, given an appropriately appreciative audience- my dear cousin Horus for instance; I considered his marriage to Asherah, but I thought- no, not yet. I wonder at times if the delight of it is held by the anticipation, the thought..."

"Oh? Oh, well, I do have a story for you! During negotiations with the buyer I thought, let us have a firearms expert examine this, and wouldn't you know that I happened to be hosting a wealthy financier who's done some work with Tri-Tachyon - and say what you will for the Company, they are good at what they do - and she happened to have a brother with an interest in a firm..."

"Eh? Ah! Well I daresay, it is you who might answer that question fully, though I've always considered myself something of a naturalist, or xeno-naturalist if you will ha-ha, and so what if I were to finance an expedition of discovery of sorts? 'Menes Yaribay, patron of knowledge'; I like the sound of that. Imagine, to explore new worlds, encounter unknown lifeforms, seek out and explore the far fringes..."

He rambles, and you make a good show of asking for additional details, drawing his attention toward the object at hand and away from whâtever in Ludd's name Bornanew is doing.

A piercing alarm interrupts Menes mid-thought. His eyes go wide and snap toward the central atrium which holds the glove- and Bornanew. He takes off at a run.

You both find Bornanew, still on his knees, with one hand stretched out to touch the glove - one finger upon it - lips still moving, eyes still blankly huge as if overcome by religious mania.

Menes gives out a wordless cry of alarm, and begins lifting his cuff to call security.

Your off-hand instinctively curls around a control interface for one of the clever little thieves-tools you have hidden about your uniform.
options:
bffi_mpSteal12a:"$global.jethroName! Restrain yourself, man!"
bffi_mpSteal12b:"He's only touched it, no need for-"

text:
Menes hesitates as you speak, then barks a codeword into his comms.

You freeze, understanding what this sort of protocol looks like. The lights flare into harsh illumination and a trio of gun turrets pop from subtle cupolas - one each tracks instantly to you and Bornanew while the third spins in a slow covering pattern.

The human guards arrive a moment later, uniforms bright-white, boots squeaking on the floor. Menes breathes heavily, watching the security net tighten.

Bornanew, meanwhile, has not moved but to turn his head toward you and Menes.
"Menes, Yaribay sir. I pray your forgiveness. I- I was overcome by... I had to..." Bornanew trails off, pathetically.
options:
bffi_mpSteal14a:Ne rien dire
bffi_mpSteal14b:(whisper) "This'll make a great story."

text:
It takes a second, but Menes starts to laugh.

And laugh, and laugh deep in his belly, almost a shout. He suddenly throws an arm over your shoulder, putting you off balance, as he struggles to contain himself.
"You're right!" he shouts, "Captain, by the stars, you are right."

"You there," Menes calls out, "Knight $global.jethroName, get yourself off the floor. Come wash your face, you look absolutely disgusting. Back to the party! Drinks are on me, ha-ha!"

As almost an afterthought he calls to the security team, "You lot, clean this up and get out of here... ruining the mood."

text:
You and Bornanew, he given a basin and washcloth by a servitor, are hauled back into the party, and the entire crowd regaled with the story of Bornanew being overcome with awe at the relic and then caught setting off the security alarm.

Menes seems to find it more hilarious with every telling - and each additional round of drinks. His dutiful entourage joins in the laughter each time. The skinny man, Ulmus Pond, has a particularly manic, piercing laugh.

It is some time before you are able to get a quiet word in with $global.jethroName.

text:
"I beg pardon for your blasphemy, captain," Bornanew says with a hint of strain in his voice. "Ludd's conception of hell was, so much as the Word reveals, a description of mortal torment in a Domain without God."

"But I do have the glove. What remains is-" an almost malicious smile briefly appears, "a fake."

"Little, but for the monotony of my humiliation, however justly earned," Bornanew says with a hint of strain in his voice. "But I do have the glove. What remains is a-" an almost malicious smile very briefly appears, "fake."
He lifts a finger to his lips, and with his other hand pats a fold in his Knight's robes.

text:
His eyes keep watch on Menes as he replies.

"It's just a glove, captain. The color matches. I didn't think Menes would notice." His mouth quirks. "He didn't."

"He will notice eventually, however. We should... become distant from this place."

* * *

It is a feat of diplomacy that you manage to extract yourself and Bornanew as quickly as you are able.

A faked containment failure alert from your chief engineer merely opens the door. Passing through requires shaking everyone's hand, exchanging pleasant words, and going through the motions of good-byes several times over - then each time the ritual is interrupted by some tangent or anecdote from Menes, he ensures that it is begun again from the start.

At last you find yourself back in your shuttle, a half-full glass still in your hand.

script:
$global.bornanewBadBoy++
$player.ethosLiar++
$player.BFFImadeBornanewBackChurchFundingLie = true

$buyerArgsMade++

text:
As you commit to the lie, Bornanew gives you a look of absolute weariness.

Menes turns to him, "This is true? This is why you're here, isn't it. The backing of the Church, of course!"

"Yes," Bornanew says robotically, his attempt at a pleasant countenance looks like an appalling mask of pain.

Even Menes seems to sense something is off. "Are you feeling well, Knight?" he asks. "Perhaps you aren't used to the quality of drink I serve."

Bornanew closes his eyes, and you hear his sigh of relief.

Menes glances at him, giving a funny look, then back at you. "Ah, of course. Ha-ha." He clears his throat. "Odd folk, you spacers," he says as if to himself.
Menes smirks, and holds out his TriPad, ready to accept your transfer code. Perhaps he feels like he's calling your bluff.
Menes smirks, and holds out his TriPad, ready to accept your transfer code. Perhaps he feels like he's calling your bluff.
Menes smirks, and holds out his TriPad, ready to accept your transfer code. Perhaps he feels like he's calling your bluff.

You tap your TriPad to his and it gives a happy little ping.

"Ah," Menes looks down to confirm. "So you are serious. I must admit, your decisiveness is impressive. Most spacer captains, they're all talk. You," he lifts his eyebrows like a conspirator, "mean business."

"But of course," Menes says, pouring on a gracious act. "Silly me, so caught up in your reputation as a space captain... having a respectable title - you must understand - it is simply unexpected."

"We're the League!" He slaps a hand across your back. "We have to stick together, you know. Or the Hegemony will get us - or, precious Ludd, the Church might roll in to force us all to pray night and day, ha!" He looks toward Bornanew, "Ha-ha! A joke, friend, a joke."

"But of course," Menes says, pouring on a gracious act. "Silly me, so caught up in your reputation as just a space captain... having a respectable title - you must understand - it is simply unexpected."

"Well, I'm sure you'll join the League soon enough!" He slaps a hand across your back. "We have to stick together, you know. Or the Hegemony will get us - or, precious Ludd, the Church might roll in to force us all to pray night and day, ha!" He looks toward Bornanew, "Ha-ha! A joke, friend, a joke."

"No doubt!" Menes exclaims, "Why, I should have commended you earlier for your service to the League. I was so caught up in your reputation as a spacer - you know, adventures and schemes and the like - it completely slipped my mind that you're on our side." 

He slaps a hand across your back. "We appreciate your work, you know. Imagine if the Hegemony came for us - or, precious Ludd, imagine if the Church rolled in to force us all to pray night and day, ha!" He looks toward Bornanew, "Ha-ha! A joke, friend, a joke."

"Rayan Arroyo, you say?" Menes perks up. "Yes, I do believe I've met the man. An honest dealer. No-nonsense. Good head on his shoulders. Fantastic taste... have you met his tailor? Stars above, a wonder! I should have them cloned."

"Well," he nods to himself, "I admit this does change my perspective. You should have mentioned him before!"

You draw your TriPad and demonstrate.

"By the stars, he did," Menes says, eyes wide. "I knew I recognized your name from somewhere! There must have been a comms sent to the upper gens about this ploy Horus was running. That's cousin Horus for you, isn't it? Always pushing his little projects."

"Well," he looks as if he's reappraising you, "If you're good enough for cousin Horus, I suppose I could be convinced to make certain allowances."

"Captain $playerName trusts you implicitly," Bornanew explains, "So as $playerHisOrHer advisor, I must act the skeptic. I assure you, Demarch Yaribay, it brings me no pleasure to appear to question and besmirch your fine reputation."

He speaks with such sincerity, for just a moment you can almost believe $global.jethroName isn't lying.

"Consider it a policy of 'trust, but verify'. We trust you, of course. But what of your source? We would never wish to see you cheated, but alas, I know too well that sin which lives in the human heart... If anything, I believe your only weakness is being too kind a spirit, too generous a benefactor."

Menes begins to nod, especially as Bornanew hits the last part of his 'explanation'.

"Yes," he exhales, "I suppose you're right. I am, if anything, too giving. This was so true on Kazeron, where the resentful and dishonest sought to tear me down and entrap me in one scandal after another."

"Out here, the social environment is more rarified, more, dare I say it? Honest. Even if it is a hopeless backwater, I do try to illuminate the locals with the light of Kazeronian culture..."
"Well, I suppose that does it!" Menes claps his hands together.

"Certainly! I'll have my secretary make the arrangements. But first - a toast to the start of what I am sure will be a bountiful partnership! Back to the party!"

"Even you," he slaps a hand on Bornanew's shoulder, pulling him along, "Surely you know how to celebrate a deal, my dour unbeliever? Perhaps not, so I'll show you!"

You and Bornanew are hauled back into the party, and the small crowd regaled with insinuations about a 'large purchase' by an unnamed 'wealthy spacer captain'.

Menes grows less discreet, winking and nudging with each retelling - and with each additional round of drinks. His dutiful entourage joins in to toast a 'new business deal' and theorize about extensions to Menes' complex.

You can see the skinny man, Ulmus Pond, watching you. He has a particularly manic, piercing laugh.

It is some time before you are able to get a quiet word in with $global.jethroName.
options:
bffi_buyerOutro2a:"What's your read on Pond?"
bffi_buyerOutro2b:"$global.jethroName. $global.jethroName, can you finish my drink? I can't."

text:
"He's a Pather agent," Bornanew says as if it were the most obvious thing in the world. He meets your look.

"We spoke briefly," he explains. "The circumstantial evidence is convincing enough, but it was his heretical understanding of theology which I found truly damning."

"I propose we retire from this, ah, celebration as soon as we can with the glove. Then investigate Pond."

"Um," Bornanew shifts uncomfortably.

"I... Yes, I can take that for you, captain." He carefully takes your drink from you without spilling, and then gently places it on a protruding piece of architecture.

"Did you bring any anti-inebriants? Perhaps we should get you back to your flagship. Here, just take my arm..."

* * *

It is a feat of diplomacy that you manage to extract yourself and Bornanew as quickly as you are able.

A faked containment failure alert from your chief engineer merely opens the door. Passing through requires shaking everyone's hand, exchanging pleasant words, and going through the motions of good-byes several times over - then each time the ritual is interrupted by some tangent or anecdote from Menes, he ensures that it is begun against from the start.

At last you find yourself back in your shuttle, a half-full glass still in your hand.

* * *

It is a feat of diplomacy that Bornanew manages to extract you as quickly as he does.

You have little difficulty playing up a state of stumbling drunkenness as Bornanew makes excuses, explaining that he will "pray for the captain's intemperance", much to the amusement of Menes' guests.

It is not your most dignified moment, but it ensures you can avoid the ritual of shaking everyone's hand, exchanging pleasant words, and going through the motions of good-byes.

options: 2:bffi_apHorus0:Shuttle down with Horus Yaribay to Menes' compound.

text:
Horus transfers to your $shipOrFleet in orbit, greeting you and Bornanew tersely then making little in the way of conversation.

Menes' invitation provides you with a flight path and Demarch-level traffic control override codes. Horus would certainly receive similar treatment had he declared his presence openly.

As it is, it's probably a violation of Persean League ethical guidelines for you to be using these to attend a personal party, but from what you can overhear of the pilot's comms, the locals have a casual attitude toward the arrangement.

Menes Yaribay's compound is a scrubbed-looking slab of trendy architecture set on a bluff overlooking Olinadu's primary habitation cluster.

A smattering of outbuildings and hangars, attached by fat trunks of conduit and glowing walktubes, sprawl across the less-fashionable view-angles of the compound toward the refineries and holding tanks of the planet's industrial center.

One set of hangar bay doors dutifully open, and your shuttle descends through the glowing atmo-field. Such gadgetry is an ostentatious addition to a personal residence, but it avoids the bother of visitors (and their shuttles) having to undergo pressure-cycling.

An enthusiastic Menes is already approaching your shuttle's access ramp as it sets itself down and signals the all-clear.

Horus Yaribay puts out his hand in a 'you first' gesture.

"Captain $playerName - with your Knight of Ludd! Let me welcome you both to my humble..."

As Menes sees Horus step off the shuttle his face contorts through a fascinating sequence of expressions before settling into an alarmed rictus of welcome.

"C-cousin Horus. What an unexpected surprise!"

Menes' eyes, watery with a minor aspect of terror, dart to yours then away before the accusation fully solidifies.

Horus' smile in return is cool as he steps forward to the offered hands with only one of his own. "Menes. Where has the last cycle gone? It feels as though your inaugural flight to Olinadu was just yesterday."

Menes manages to respond with an obliging laugh.

Bornanew, quite forgotten, follows you and the Yaribays.

Menes leads your party past gilded statues guarding the portal to the main complex; the forms are evocative of splashes of molten gold frozen by happenstance into sensual humanoid figures.

You watch as Horus, lean and intense as some small predator, endures the patter from Menes, a man of more generous form and character. Menes plays down the 'frontier quality' of renovations, the poor selection of artworks, the difficulty of accessing the best artists and engineering firms to contribute to his home in 'all but exile'.

Occasionally a robotic or human servitor appears to offer drinks or nibbles, and you've a growing feeling that Menes has picked up a glass from one only to place it back on the tray of another without ever taking a sip.

A fresh glass in hand, Menes tentatively leads Horus into what can only be his prize entertaining space.

The space is at once cavernous and intimate; the sightlines and carved, wrought, or woven details calculated to replicate a feeling of cozy warmth like the den of some thoughtful patron of art, history, and literature.

A double handful of well-dressed remoras mill about, interrupting their conversations to turn as Menes enters. They look to be a collection of industrialists, financiers, distant gens-relatives, and at least one officer from atop the local military detachment who appears a little worse for his drink.

Most start to give a politic nod and wave of acknowledgement at Menes' entrance, but stop and stare as they recognize Horus at his side. Few eyes linger on you and Bornanew, who steps apart as though he were a mere assistant or servitor.

One skinny man in a finely embroidered suit is not put off by Horus' entrance, and rushes to greet your party, eyes agog mostly at the gens patriarch, but sparing a glance at least for the newest additions to Menes' collection.

He nearly trips over himself to speak. "The great Horus Yaribay! Your reputation precedes you, if half of what-"

Horus holds up a hand, silencing the man, and turns aside. "Menes, what is this?"

"Ha- ha, ah, this is Ulmus Pond," Menes replies quickly, his drinkless off-hand waving to shoo Pond away. "Ulmus, not now. Perhaps see that Captain $playerLastName's Luddic friend here has a fresh drink? Thank-you."

"Now, cousin Horus, I must offer a thousand apologies." Menes himself turns, also cutting Pond out of the conversation. "Had you signalled ahead we could have arranged for a proper reception..."

script:
AdjustRepPerson ulmus_pond SUSPICIOUS -1
HideThirdPerson
$option = bffi_apHorus9b 0
FireBest DialogOptionSelected
text:
"Never mind that," Horus says, looking around with a displeased curl to his lip. His gaze slips over Pond like he's nothing.

Ulmus Pond, for his part, hides the evil look that crossed his face in that moment to slink after Bornanew.

Horus snaps his fingers, summoning a tray, and selects something effervescent. He takes a slow sip, not allowing his expression to display any sort of pleasure in the act. It seems to him more like a necessary palate-cleanser.

"Show it to me," he says to Menes. An order.

"Captain $playerLastName shall attend," Horus adds quietly. "So that we might fully resolve this matter."

"Of course, cousin, of course!" Menes, sweating, puts on a fragile grin in order to distribute reassuring nods to his extended entourage.

Menes leads you and Horus into his inner sanctum, flashing a grimace as he operates a series of obtrusive and, to your eyes, largely ornamental biometric checks.

The air cools by a degree, perhaps two, the armored mass of the walls felt as a dullness through your feet. The subdued lighting, drapery, and framing of the space suggest the interior of a bank vault or a shrine, though not to the praise of any Luddic virtue.

Quite the contrary, depending on one's outlook.

On a pedestal of carved wood, in a spot of light like holy revelation, a single glove is upthrust. Its fingers appear frozen in the midst of beginning to curl back toward a relaxed state after giving you a little wave.

After an appropriate pause, Menes speaks up, hopeful. "What do you think, cousin?"

"What's this supposed to be," says Horus, deadpan.

Menes clears his throat, then begins in a low, dramatic voice, "The glove worn by Ludd when stepping upon the sacred Earth of Gilead. Ludd removed the glove and kissed the ground, according to the Luddic's religion."
options: bffi_apHorus14:"And it's almost certainly a fake."

text:
Menes looks shocked.

Horus gives you a look of his own. "Of course it is," he says. "And here I thought only a Luddie would be foolish enough to believe in this sort of thing."

"Cousin!" Menes exclaims, appalled.

"Do not 'cousin' me!" Horus shouts, now, after an icy restraint held for so long.

"Remember how I saved you last time? The promises you made? And here, you've wasted stars-know how many credits on more nonsense, a bomb waiting to explode under the gens!"
options: bffi_apHorus15:Keep your mouth shut, for now

text:
"What would Reynard Hannan do if he knew what you were up to here, do you think?"

Menes shakes his head, speechless.

"He would loose the Church on us! And their bloody-minded Pathers! Think, think for once, 'cousin'." Horus spits the word poisonously. "Think of anyone in the Sector aside from yourself. Think of your gens!"

"The Chu- Path?" Menes sputters pathetically. "If Captain $playerLastName said it's a fake, it shouldn't be-"

"Oh, so now it's a fake?" Horus cuts him off. "Enough! Here is what will happen."

"Captain $playerLastName and $playerHisOrHer pet Knight of Ludd shall take possession of the damnable trinket. Immediately. As a sign of good faith to our Luddic 'friends' in the Church of Galactic Redemption."

He gives you a look, "See that they appreciate what I've done for them."

Back to Menes. "From now on I am taking personal control of your accounts. No frivolities without approval from my office."

Menes begins to make a sound of objection but is cut off as Horus raises a single finger. A Reaper torpedo, armed and ready to launch into the unshielded breach.

"You and I," Horus says, "Shall continue our conversation in private." He doesn't turn to you again. "Captain, if you would kindly?"

Despite knowing what it is - or, rather, isn't - it feels almost like sacrilege to simply reach over to touch this object of such presumed expense and such evident disgrace. A vessel assigned such power and import, faith and violence, despite its reified deceit.

It is the glowing prize at the center of this temple, this shrine to the ego of Menes Yaribay bought with the lucre of the Kazeronian empire, fueled by faith and blood from over two hundred cycles since someone who took the name Ludd did something, probably, half-remembered then gilded in glorious myth on a planet named Gilead.
options:
bffi_apHorus19:Just take the glove already
bffi_apHorus18:Hesitate

text: The quiet of the air circulation looms. Horus clears his throat.

The alarm stops, leaving a painful echo in your ears.

He simply nods, placing his hands together. He doesn't do anything more, but you swear he's praying.
"I did," he says ruefully.

He accepts the glove, taking only a split second to suppress his look of shock. Bornanew quickly disappears the glove to some hidden pocket of his robes.

"I think, perhaps," he says in a whisper, "that should have remained hidden."
options:

bffi_apHorus23g:"Don't worry. Menes is going to be on his best good behavior from here on out."

text: Bornanew spares you a single rueful look.

On a pedestal of carved wood, in a spot of light like holy revelation, a single glove is upthrust. Its fingers appear frozen in the midst of just starting to curl back toward a relaxed state after giving you a little wave.

"Do you know what this is?" Menes whispers to you. 

"It looks like a glove, demarch," Bornanew answers in feigned misunderstanding, stepping forward.

"Not any glove," Menes says with more than a hint of vexation.

He begins again in a reverent whisper, "The glove worn by Ludd upon stepping upon the sacred Earth of Gilead. Ludd removed the glove and kissed the ground, according to the Luddic religion. Isn't that right, sir Knight? And this... is it."

Bornanew looks at you, his eyebrows rising. "Could it be?" he asks, allowing some melodrama to enter his voice.
options:
bffi_mpRaid2:"I'm impressed, Menes. A relic from a pivotal moment of the Sector's history!"
bffi_mpCallRaid:Call in the raid

text:
"Precisely my reaction upon first hearing word of this unique artifact!" Menes beams, more than a little pleased that someone appreciates his trinket.

"Setting aside its purely religious significance," he gives a glance at Bornanew, "it represents a, a heralding of the changing tides of history! The ebbing influence of the Domain-era megacorporations, the rising of what some call - not my words of course, sir Knight - an atavistic populism. Truly, it is this small piece of the vastness of the story of Humanity which called to me when it was first described to me..."

Menes seems prepared to continue this for quite some time.
Menes is blathering on about something or other while Bornanew pretends to listen.
Menes is blathering on about something or other while Bornanew pretends to listen.

This isn't working. It's a good thing you prepared a backup plan.

You'll simply call in a marine raid while keeping Menes distracted. You give a grim nod to Bornanew and with only a small pause he follows your lead, asking Menes an insightful question about another item in his collection.

text:
You glance at your comms. Everything has been planned ahead of time, and care taken to hide tactical teams among the general population of offworld crew taking a bit of shore-leave. Signal discipline has been unerring.

The coded response, hidden in routine status reports, shows all teams are prepped and ready to move.

Menes is describing another of his expensive acquisitions when the lights cut out.

A moment passes in absolute darkness, the crushing weight of silence falling as the ventilation whirs to a stop. This is the terrible abyss of spacers' death; a reminder that you exist by the whim of machines caught in deadly vacuum, in a precarious balance of temperature and artificial atmosphere.

The subdued red emergency lighting strips flicker to life. Menes produces a nervous smile, "I'm sure it's just, um, a glitch." He mumbles something into his cuff, then gives it a confused shake.

Comms down. By the plan.

"It's probably- I'm certain- I, I," Menes stutters, "The power will come back soon. Those rad-addled Olinadu contractors," he attempts a nervous laugh.

You feel the dull thuds, nearly subsonic, through the armored mass of the vault. Breaching charges for the first layer of defense. You know the big one will come soon, and where.

Menes is in a growing panic.
options: bffi_mpRaidOutro2:"Perhaps, Menes, we should take cover..."

text:
When the marines finally breach the vault proper, it is with overwhelming force of sound and light. No unprotected human can withstand this assault on the senses; you are blinded, shocked, and rendered immobile. All you can do is cling to the kernel of self at the center of a storm of pain.

* * *

The return is slow, an awakening from a terrible and drugged sleep to emergency medical personnel and Menes' somewhat tardy security teams in their gleaming armor.

You and Bornanew leave the compound among the other victims. Bandaged, bruised, head aching, ears ringing.

script:

text:
Hours later, after a series of transfers to obscure your movements from the heightened official surveillance, you step back onto your own shuttle - and the attentions of your personal medical team.

A chime on your TriPad confirms that the operation was a success. A holo of the simple work-glove that human beings fought and died over rotates mockingly. 

"Evidence," Bornanew says, tired but alert. His eyes are alarmingly bloodshot. "The seed of schism and war. Now whence did this come?"
options:
bffi_mpRaidOutro4a:"I don't suppose we could ask Menes."
bffi_mpRaidOutro4b:"What did you make of that Ulmus Pond?"

text:
"No," he manages a smile for just a moment. "I would suppose not."

He looks back down at the holo.

"A Pather agent," he says conclusively.

"We spoke briefly," he says. "In the aftermath. He insisted the medical team check him. Wasn't hurt." 

Bornanew takes a deep breath, wincing. "But he slipped up. Heretical theology. Devil in the details. You can tell where one learned the Word, or, rather, a corruption of the Word, from the details."

"He may be connected, but first we should ask the glove." He looks back down at the holo.

"A nanoforge engineer could tell us much of this object," he says. "Who made it. When, and where. By the truths found in the false glove, we may judge answers we receive from those we... ask." His last word is muted by the shuttle's maneuverings.

"The Church's nanoforge is on Asher. Canaan system. The techs there will submit to the authority I bear. Our inquiry will be secret."

He pauses, and looks away. "So far as anything is, in the Church."
options: bffi_mpRaidOutro6:"We should confirm our hard evidence before Menes puts the pieces together here."

text:

The shuttle approaches your flagship and prepares to dock.

text:
"$PlayerSirOrMadam." They salute and step back so the med tech can finish waving a scanner - the high-pitched hum just inside your hearing - in search of broken bones. $global.jethroName undergoes a similar procedure in the next bay.

Before the hour you are discharged with a smart stimpack strapped to your side, under your uniform jacket, to control the pain and keep you alert.

You're not out yet. You can easily see that the local authorities don't discover Menes' trinket if your $shipOrFleet is searched, but that search would waste valuable time and disrupt operations.

text:
A joyless smirk flits over his face. 

"But you were not. The raiders were careful, very careful. Careful." He stares at you for a long moment, breathing heavily. Into his drink, perhaps?

"The glove is not the point, my dear captain." He frowns. "My sanctuary was violated. My things stolen. My... friends assaulted." He stares at you for a long moment, breathing heavily. Into his drink, perhaps?

text:
Bornanew assists your comms officer in navigating through various levels of Church bureaucracy.

Before long, you have the senior nanoforge engineer of Asher on a comms-link.

"Yes, hello? Uh, good day? I was told there was an investigation." 

She seems to examine you through the link. "You don't look much like a Knight of Ludd."
options: bffi_talkToEngineer2:"I need an object analyzed. Very closely."

text:
"I see." $PersonName takes a moment more to examine you, humming to herself.

"Forensic material analysis. Skilled work." $HeOrShe frowns. "It won't be cheap."

She looks at some other display and starts tapping at an input. "You'll have to bring the sample down, of course."

Bornanew's authority - that of the Archcurate Sophronia Jaspis, in truth - is as good with the local traffic authority as it is with the Manufactoria Guild bureaucrats.

Your shuttle descends into the atmosphere of Asher. The view shades toward a warm, murky gray as you descend through the stratospheric fines perpetually refreshed by towering dust storms. The display blots out entirely and switches to a false-rendering as you enter the lower atmosphere.

There is no hazard; the storms are impressive in scale but low in mass due to the deathly thin atmosphere.

Senior Engineer Tanaica places the glove and its containment vessel onto a shelf which retracts into the towering white casing of the analysis suite. Transparent panes, probably diamond-matrix, reveal arcane inner workings.

She hits a command to evacuate the atmo from the sample slot, and sets a manipulator to extract the glove and place it on the scanning bed. All of this she performs with a casual ease born of practice. She speaks as she taps away at the control interface.

"If it came from a high-quality nanoforge like what the Heggies or League's got, this might not tell us much." 

Some kind of protective barrier shunts down, hiding the glove from view. A holo of the glove emerges from a display unit, gaining resolution until a doppelganger rotates in the air before you.

"Hm. Good output, this. But I'm thinking not good-perfect. Ludd...ics," her eyes dart at Bornanew, "No offense, but they don't go in for training much native talent. S'why you lot pay me."

"Anyway, when you get into really delicate products, your forge quality starts to matter. A work-rated vac-glove though?" She rolls her eyes. "This better be good."

The analysis suite is making some kind of humming sound, and there's a clunk. The engineer doesn't react, so neither do you. Her display erupts with numbers and icons, then something she does pulls a cutaway from the holo-glove, which scales as the rest of it disappears. 

Another segmentation of scale iterates, then another.

"We can see signs of what we call 'drift' here," she says. A colored indicator ring flashes around an orderly pattern of small-scale structures deep within the glove, the representation of individual atoms, perhaps, just visible like the grain of sandstone.

"These defects are unique to the faults in an individual nanoforge. More or less." 

"Like a fingerprint," Bornanew says.

"No." The engineer shrugs. "But sure, if you like."

"Someone's been naughty," she says, sounding more delighted than upset. "The glove is new, but the model is old. A closed-source pre-Collapse template. There are fabrication-rights codes embedded in its structure."

With a tap, another colored indicator ring flashes on a sheared-square section of material containing a complex pattern. "Tells the forge not to make unauthorized copies."

"Closed products from the Domain-era tend to be far higher quality than forges running post-Collapse hacks." She smiles, "This isn't that, but it's close. Tells us two things."

"One, this was made by an industrial-grade Domain-era forge. Heavy industry stuff. Bit of a waste to use it to make a glove," she glances at you, "If you ask me."

"Two, either this forge is loaded with valid fabrication rights and it's damaged, or it's been hacked to bypass the embedded copy protections. And it's damaged. I mean, stars and ruin, look at this mess," she zooms in on the 'drift' patterns while making a disgusted look.

$global.jethroName speaks up now, "Three, we're dealing with a sophisticated forgery operation."

The engineer shoots a look back at him, "Neat."
options: bffi_talkToEngineer9:"What forge made this? Where's it from?"

text:
"Right," she manipulates the interface again.

The drift-pattern from the glove remains locked, and she brings up similar - but distinct - examples of patterns in an expanding grid. Small labels show a string of glyphs, most of which contain the names of industrial combines, shipyards, and polities known to you. Some you recognize as dating to the Collapse. Others are unfamiliar.

"This is the drift library we've got here on Asher. Useful stuff, but other factions got better."
options: bffi_talkToEngineer10:"So you can match the 'drift' to the forge."

text: "Exactly," she's pleased that you put it together. "Got bad news though, cap. Your glove doesn't match anything in this library."
options:
bffi_talkToEngineer11a:"Can drift be obscured, faked?"
bffi_talkToEngineer11b:"So it's not a known forge."

text: "Could be," she admits. "Takes a clever setup and a talented operator though. If this is fake drift, then it goes real high up. Don't know who'd be capable of it that'd waste that kind of effort on a glove."

"Maybe, maybe not," she says.

"The Church doesn't put much into material analysis. What you'd do, ideally, is collect statistically significant samples, run scans on every new product that shows up, put anything interesting through a detailed scan like this, build a library. It's not cheap," she says wistfully.

"But here they're mostly interested in making sure their hyperdrives work to min-spec. The Church of No-fun." She looks at Bornanew, "Sorry."

He nods, untroubled.

options: bffi_talkToEngineer11b:"So it's not a known forge."

text:
"That's all I got, captain," she says, reversing the specimen insertion procedure and returning your containment vessel. "Good mystery. Hope it doesn't get you shot."

She hands the vessel to Bornanew, then claps her hands together. "That'll be 10,000 credits."

options: bffi_talkToEngineer13corruption:"You serve the Church. Is this not corruption, $jethroName?"

text:

"Didn't you tell your captain about the fee?" She asks.

You'd swear, Bornanew is almost blushing.

You transfer the credits to Senior Engineer Tanaica's accounts while $global.jethroName holds the vessel.

Presumably she'll forward payment for lab expenses to the Church-approved Manufactoria Guild.

Presumably she'll forward payment for lab expenses to the Church-approved Manufactoria Guild. Or not.

"I... yes, the captain is correct. You are paid to serve the Church, and this," he hefts the ersatz reliquary, "is business of the Church."

"Right you are," says Senior Engineer Tanaica through a tight smile. "Good day, captain."

Her eyes flick at Bornanew. "Novice."

Bornanew's tone was righteous, but without heart. You suspect he was not going to make an issue of the 'payment' until you brought it up.

As you return to your shuttle he gives you a look- guilt?

"Such things are not uncommon even in the shadow of the Church," he says. "And elsewhere. I'm sure you well know."

Bornanew returns his attention to his datapad. He's making his 'thinking face'."

He recovers quickly. "I, of course," he awkwardly hands the ersatz reliquary to you then draws an off-brand datapad from his robes. Engineer $personLastName blinks at it.

"I can pay from the, uh, accounts of The Reverend Archcurate Sophronia Jaspis... here."

You return to your shuttle. 

Bornanew's attention is uncharacteristically focused on his datapad. He's making his 'thinking face'.

"Ulmus Pond," he says. Then looks up at you.

"That strange little man from Menes Yaribay's party. Obviously an agent for the Luddic Path. That the Path would have eyes and influence on Menes is no surprise. But if he's the one that supplied the glove..."

"We need to get to him," he says. "Confront him. Compel him to talk."

text: You step off the shuttle.

The comms signal hits a holding signal and waits. And waits. You have your comms officer take over to watch the link and make a few inquiries.

Within minutes, a comms request alert appears. It's Bornanew on an internal link.

"Captain," he says. "I apologize for the interruption."

"Ulmus Pond is not on Olinadu."
options: bffi_ulmusPondNotHere2:"You're sure. How do you know this?"

text: "The Knights have... sources," he says carefully. "I am sworn not to reveal them."

"Yes." Bornanew clears his throat.

"The word is that he has fled to Chalcedon on a tramp freighter, a smuggler, known to move materiel for the Luddic Path."
options:
bffi_ulmusPondNotHere4a:"Does an innocent man run?"
bffi_ulmusPondNotHere4b:"Then we follow."

text: He turns his head. "He might, if he had to. But I suspect that Ulmus is not an innocent man."
"I pray that if we move quickly, we can find him on Chalcedon."

options: bffi_upNotHereAskRole:"What's Ulmus Pond's role? What does finding him do for us?"
bffi_upNotHereSourcesB:"What if these are pirates, or a major faction misleading us?"
bffi_upNotHereSourcesA:"Before we act, let us speak of your Knights' 'sources'."
bffi_upNotHereIntentions:"Could your queries have revealed our intentions?"

text: "He would be - have been - well placed as a bridge for some element of the Luddic Path to influence Menes Yaribay. He would be the source of the relic."

"I cannot," he says. "But I assure you they are as reliable as any heretic, guilty soul, or criminal." His look softens, "Do trust that I am not simply asking around a spacer bar for a lead."

A moment, then he gives a nod, almost a bow. "Um. I do not condemn your methods. Your directness has oft proven effectual."

"It could be either." Bornanew folds his hands together. "But we must look first where the light of truth shines brightest, dim though it may be, or known only by distinction against the shadow of deceit."

"It is always possible." Bornanew meets your eyes, steady. "The Knights pursue heretics and criminals as routine, and my inquiries were worded to imply my interest lay not upon Ulmus Pond himself, nor the Menes situation. Though, of course, you and I are already known to be associated with both."

"Expediency anticipates risk. I beg you trust my judgment in this matter."
