text:
Chalcedon rotates in the primary display before you.

Bornanew is at your side, tapping at his off-brand datapad, looking for all the worlds like a toy in his large hands.

"My sources indicate that a man matching the description of Ulmus Pond is almost certainly in a safehouse... here."

A highlighted point appears on the globe, some logistical center in the scablands trailing like a stain from the primary spaceport.

Bornanew watches the point as the globe obligingly comes to a gentle stop. The highlighted section detaches from the globe, which collapses away, and spreads into a tactical overview.

"He acts like a man drowning, grasping for help without concern for his rescuers." He turns from the globe toward you, "In OPSEC terms, of course. He could have easily disappeared."

A bitter smile appears, "As once I did."
options: bffi_ulmusPrestart3:"Tactical, prep a shuttle. Let's have a chat with Ulmus Pond."

script:
ShowLargePlanet
SetTooltip bffi_ulmusPondYouBad "Due to his criminal background, $global.jethroName must be familiar with the art of intimidation. He would perform the role well."
SetTooltip bffi_ulmusPondMeBad "In consideration of his criminal background, you don't want to put $global.jethroName back into playing a role he's worked so hard to leave behind."
text:
Bornanew has his notebook and cheap old datapad packed in a satchel made of what looks like leather. You've heard of the primitive process the Luddics are said to use to produce the material; the skin of an animal is stripped then stewed, somehow, with its own waste products. It sounded unbearably primitive when described. Anti-Luddic misinformation, perhaps, because you don't detect the smell of feces at all.

"How shall we approach this?" Bornanew asks. "Like in MuniSec dramas- one of us is friendly to the subject, the other hostile?"
options:
bffi_ulmusPondMeBad:"I'll be the bad guy."
bffi_ulmusPondYouBad:"You be the bad guy."

text: Bornanew nods. Perhaps you're imagining it, but is that relief in the set of his eyes?
Bornanew nods. Perhaps you're imagining it, but you feel like a coldness comes into the set of his eyes.
Your personal security team has been briefed, and are dressed as a mix of common spacers, local techs, and Luddic laborers. Their arsenal is multitude and well-concealed.
Your own hand finds your trusty sidearm, a matte polycarbonate pistol. An ideal firearm for use in tight spaces, its mass rests securely in your holster.
notes: # default

text: You, of course, have your clutch of devilish little grenades built to resist milliradar detection. Fearful and unexpected, they trigger with a practiced twitch from your hand.
And you have hidden a vicious ceramic vibroknife on your person. It is not a tool to be underestimated, for when déployéd by surprise it can even disable light power-armored opponents.

You are protected by a subtler weapon, a tiny capsule of spray-poison within your cuff. Hold your breath as it is déployéd with a casual wave, and the target next to you will simply die as if of a massive, unexpected heart-attack.

You, of course, have a monomolecular wire hidden in a false fingertip, ready to deploy into an invisible cutting edge with but a short, sharp twist. It is a venerable weapon favored by assassins of a certain class.

You, of course, need trust nothing more than your limbs. Starship combat is mass and energy applied at scale; absurdly reduced to the scale of the human body, the mechanics applied are not completely dissimilar.
Your hand finds the hilt of your officers' sword at your side, the mass of a steel blade a comfort shared with warriors dating to the primitive conflicts of Old Earth.

You stand with Bornanew in an uncomfortably narrow alley. The sky above sends nasty rivulets of fluid down the conduits and moldy canopies that diffuse the light into a flattening murk.

Your security team is invisible, discreet in nearby alcoves and rooftops.

$global.jethroName runs his hands over a door, as if feeling under it's moisture-warped surface for a clue.
"Ready, captain?" he asks. "Remember, you're the good guy."
options:
bffi_ulmusStart3:"I'm ready."
bffi_ulmusStart3a:"You're sure he's here?"

text: "Ready, captain?" he asks. "Remember, you're the bad guy."
options:
bffi_ulmusStart3:"Don't you forget it."
bffi_ulmusStart3a:"You're sure he's here?"

text: "I am," he says.
Bornanew takes a step back then slams a powerful leg into a worn spot, about waist-high. With a sharp crack, the entire door falls inward, crumbling hinges and locking mechanism still attached.

Bornanew charges inside. You follow his steps through an entryway, plastic rain cloaks mildewing, through a prefab kitchen and wash unit stained the yellow of old teeth. A sitting area, chair toppled. There's a muffled thump from above.

Bornanew glances at you, his eyes giving a bounce upwards. Ulmus Pond.

A moment of frantic search reveals the narrow stairwell behind a loose wall-panel.

Bornanew pounds up the stairs, and you follow.

In his half-second lead, Bornanew has caught Ulmus Pond in a bearhug, Pond's skinny arms still grabbing at the loosened attachment-points of a sheet of translucent plastic which lies between him and the lightwell; escape. Pond loses his grip with a cry of despair as Bornanew pulls him away.

The window panel snaps back into place.

id:
# BFFI Ulmus
# Bornanew is Good cop

text:
"Ulmus Pond," Bornanew roars with overbearing gusto, "This is no season to be opening a window!"

"Sit, I insist, before you hurt yourself! We are doubly blessed to find you so well. You must understand our fear- no answer to comms, rumors of kidnappings... Menes has been worried sick about his very good friend."

The bear-hug has turned into something like a side-grab as Bornanew sets Pond bodily onto the cot beside him. You're not sure if the creaking is from the cheap furniture or Pond' ribs.
options:
bffi_ulmusTalkBad1a:"More than worried. He suspects you, Pond. We're going to get answers."
bffi_ulmusTalkBad1b:"Easy $global.jethroName. Ulmus only had good intentions I'm sure."

text:
"A- answers?" Pond's eyes go wider still as he gasps for breath. "I, um, appreciate your concern but I'm fine, here. Nothing is the matter, truly!"

"Look at him, captain," Bornanew says. "He's an innocent. He'd tell us straightaway if some ill-intentioned party were taking advantage of his good nature."
options:
bffi_ulmusTalkBad2a:"No, I think Ulmus is behind it. We should do an active scan on his brain."
bffi_ulmusTalkBad2b:"Perhaps you're right, he does look pretty innocent."

text:
"Ulmus?" Bornanew looks at you, shocked at the idea. 

"...'active' scan?" Ulmus manages to squeak.

"The captain would never," insists Bornanew. "You're a merchant, a middleman. You just wanted to help your friend Menes find a unique object for his collection. Isn't that right?" He gives Ulmus another squeeze.

"Y-yes, that's right," Ulmus says, looking between you and Bornanew. "I, uh, I set up connections. Deals. I help people!"
options: bffi_ulmusTalkBad3a:"So you admit you 'helped' Menes buy a fake relic from the Pathers!"

text:
Bornanew tips his head, giving you a confused look. 

"The good captain here thought someone," he pauses to give you a consternated expression, "-might be taking advantage of Menes. $PlayerHeOrShe wants to eliminate all suspects."

"I, for one, insisted to Menes that you must be the victim of some terrible scheme, and that I would pray for you, and, better yet, as a Knight of Ludd carry out my sworn duty to protect the faithful and innocent alike."

Bornanew gives Pond a squeeze to emphasize how safe he is, now.
options:
bffi_ulmusTalkBad2a:"Ulmus is behind it. We should perform an active scan on his brain."
bffi_ulmusTalkBad2b:"On second thought, I don't think Ulmus had anything to do with it."

text:
Bornanew tips his head, giving you a confused look.

"The captain here told me," he pauses to give you a consternated expression, "-he thought you were working for Pathers, to sell Menes a fake relic to cause discord and make off with his credits. The captain told me $playerHeOrShe wanted to test an invasive brain-interfacer $playerHeOrShe found in some ruin on the fringe to discover the truth inside your head."

Pond's eyes go wide at the mention of 'brain-interface'.

"I, for one," Bornanew says reassuringly, "insisted that the plan was crude and unnecessary. Surely you're the victim of a terrible scheme..."

bffi_ulmusTalkBad3a:"Ah... yes, that's right. We really should try the brain-interfacer."
bffi_ulmusTalkBad3b:"'Brain-interfacer'? What is this nonsense $global.jethroName?"

text:
"I didn't know they were Pathers!" Pond shouts.

Bornanew pretends shock at the notion, looking at Ulmus and shaking his head.

"Or that it was a fake! I swear, I'm a legitimate entrepreneur! I've been set up. I have enemies, Menes has enemies, they- they must have set me up!"

text:
Bornanew tips his head, giving you a confused look.

"Now's not the time to play coy, captain. Your illegal brain-interfacer," he stresses the words, "which you proposed could 'scientifically prove' Pond's connection to a Pather network with only 'minimal damage'."

Pond looks rapidly between you and Bornanew, both scared and confused. "Minimal da-" he begins, then shifts course to avoid the subject entirely. "I didn't know they were Pathers!" he pleads.

"Or that it was a fake! I swear, I'm a legitimate entrepreneur. There's no need for brain-scans or, or- I've been set up! I have enemies, Menes has enemies, they- they must have set me up!"
options: bffi_ulmusTalkBad4a:"But you ran, Ulmus. Right back to your Pather masters here on Chalcedon."

text:
"I-I had no... you don't understand," Ulmus squirms.

Bornanew jumps in to direct Ulmus' narrative. "He must have been threatened," he says. "Blackmail! Of course. You help us find out who's really behind this! We'll make sure you're safe."
options: bffi_ulmusTalkBad5a:"If, and only if, $global.jethroName, he helps us. Otherwise he goes to the Inquisitors."

text:
"Surely there is no need to bring them in it," Bornanew puts on a look of saintly compassion.

"I know in my heart that Ulmus wants to confess and be absolved of his sins, of his crimes. He wants to leave this terrible time behind him and live a good life."

Pond's eyes go teary. "Y-yes, yes. I'll confess! I'll do penance, I'll pray! I'll tell you whâtever you need. By Ludd, I beg forgiveness!"

Pond sniffles.

"There was... a Walker of the Path. He asked me to talk to Menes, to, to, to make the offer. He said Menes couldn't resist an artifact of such quality. And- and I was to arrange transport. Then make it appear legitimate. Set the documents straight, make it look correct."

Bornanew draws his cheap datapad from his satchel, the screen already set to a map of the greater Persean Sector.

"Are you man, or a bit of vermin slipping out the window?" Bornanew bellows as he lifts Pond bodily, setting him on a sagging cot. You're not sure if the creak is the cheap furniture or Pond's ribs.

text:
Pond's eyes go large and pleading- with his escape closed off, you are his only salvation.

"Yes, yes," he speaks quickly. "The captain has it right! Menes, he wanted something special. I'm just a middleman, a, a communicator. I just make deals!"

He licks his lips, looking between you and Bornanew, "Menes was so pleased, I had no idea it wasn't real. I'm no relic-dealer or, or a curate or- you have to believe me, I'm innocent!"

Pond's eyes go large, his face pale, his breaths coming in sharp gasps.

Bornanew tips his head at Pond and gives you a confused and consternated look.

text:
Bornanew sighs deeply, dropping his previous countenance, and looks at you with almost a sadness in his eyes. "This is not as we agreed. I ask now that you stand aside and allow me to complete this unsavory task."

With that, his face goes blank and he turns, seizing Pond by the neck with one hand and lifting him off the cot with a choked sound.
"What, it seems, I must," he says flatly.

"Ulmus," he growls and, with his other hand Bornanew lifts his cheap datapad to within reach of Pond's flailing arms. The screen is already set to a map of the greater Persean Sector.

"Show us where the glove came from. Then you may breathe."

Bornanew's datapad trembles in Pond's hands. He cannot designate a star system until Bornanew concedes by lowering him back onto the cot.

Gasping and choking, Pond marks a system. It is difficult to make out his words. Something about "pather".

"This Pather, Ulmus," Bornanew says, glancing at the datapad then placing it in his satchel. "Tell us his name."

Tears stream down Ulmus' face; the man looks deflated, and absolutely defeated. Then he looks up and his eyes go wide.

"Sedge!" he rasps.

In one smooth motion you draw your sidearm and place a shot in Sedge's head.

With a sigh, his body slides back against the wall, his eyes blank, his expression caught in a look of dumb surprise.

In one smooth motion you draw a grenade, sliding a finger along just-so to set yield to minimum, and gently toss it toward Sedge's head. You see the beginning of a confused start on his face before the flash, and a loud clap uncomfortable almost to the point of pain.

Sedge slides back against the wall, his face caught in the eternal surprise of death. Blood begins to trickle from his nose, his eyes, his ears, his mouth. The skin of his face blooms red. It will look like a bloated purple mass in only minutes.

text:
In one smooth motion you draw your sword and sweep it in an arc intersecting Sedge's throat.

With a wet sigh, his body slides back against the wall, his face down, his eyes staring blankly up at you, his expression caught in a look of dumb surprise.

In a motion born of long hours of training, your leg snaps toward Sedge at the speed of thought. It meets the center of his head with full force, cracking his head back in a violent jerk.

A single shudder passes through his body, from his thick neck, already purpling from devastated bone and tissue, to his booted feet. His head slumps to the side at an unnatural angle, blood running from what remains of his nose, and his mouth and eyes.

text:
Bornanew lives, by a thread.

In the moments since the exchange, a time hollowed out by violence and filled with the fleeting intensity of adrenaline, your security team appears.

Finding you unhurt, they attach stabilization packs and trauma strips to the fallen Knight. He's left an incredible pool of blood in the cheap apartment. It drips down the narrow stairs.

The other two bodies lie dead.

It is unclear how fully Ulmus Pond was exploited by or truly believed in the Pather cause, but his loyalty - if he ever had any - died with him.

You carry Bornanew's leather satchel to the shuttle. Your team carries his unconscious body. 

As it lifts, he is laid across several seats. The designated medic keeps close watch over the small ad-hoc network of medical machines.

Bornanew's datapad opens to your touch, allowing you to access the coordinates given by Ulmus Pond. And a recording of the brief interrogation. $global.jethroName planned for the possibility of his incapacitation, or death. And gave you access to his work.

"A marine raid to seize datastores, comms buffers, and prisoners. Then the nanoforge and all of its support equipment should be destroyed."

Bornanew gives you a weary look.

"You may jest, and I understand your need." He turns back to the tactical display. "However I shan't close my eyes to the nature of my duty; you have taught me that much. Violence is among a Knight's tools, and I must not cower from its use."

"Consider: a marine raid to seize datastores, comms buffers, and prisoners. Then, you have demolitions experts? The nanoforge and all of its support equipment must be destroyed."

"You cannot truly know until you ask," says Bornanew, choosing to entertain your presumed levity. "The Creator gave even the Path free will, and I would accept peaceable surrender if given."

He turns to you. "And violence is among the tools of a Knight, but it must be that: a tool, not an end unto itself, 'lest it fill my heart with wrath, my soul with hâte, to thereafter leave no home for love of the Creator, nor the Word.'"

"Consider: a marine raid to seize datastores and comms buffers. Then," he examines the tactical display, "you have demolitions experts? The nanoforge and all of its support equipment must be destroyed."

"Perhaps," says Bornanew. "But do not let the fearsome reputation sown by the Luddic Path blind you to what they truly are - human. Fearful and fallible as you or I."

"They might be convinced to reveal something. Or if you can acquire comms buffers, they may prove illuminating." He looks at you, "I believe there is a mole, an agent loyal to the Path within the Holy Cathedral of Exodus with access to the vaults. I need to narrow down my list of suspects."

"To say nothing of destroying this nanoforge and any data on the glove held in datacores." He considers a moment, then says "Metadata from the scan of the glove may be useful, also."

"Of that I admit I have little doubt." Bornanew gives you a weary look.

He turns back to the tactical display. "I shan't close my eyes to the nature of my duty; you have taught me that much. Violence is among a Knight's tools, and I must not cower from its use."

"Consider: a marine raid to seize datastores, comms buffers, and prisoners. Then... you have demolitions experts? The nanoforge and all of its support equipment must be destroyed."

options: bffi_psaConHubRespCarefully:"'Carefully," isn't enough. You suggest we talk to them?"
bffi_psaConHubRespE:"I shall proceed with the operation."
bffi_psaConHubRespTalk:"Perhaps we can talk our way through this."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlast:"We could simply blast this station apart and sift the wreckage for datastores and prisoners."

text:
"I apologize for dissembling," he looks down for a moment, then back to you. "Talk? Perhaps," he says.

"Don't let the fearsome reputation of the Luddic Path blind you to what they are - as human and fallible as you or I. We were given free will, and I would accept peaceable surrender if it were given."

"That is unlikely, however. Consider: a marine raid to seize datastores and comms buffers. Then... you have demolitions experts? The nanoforge itself must be destroyed."

He thinks for a moment.

"I pray it is so. It would require some exceptional ruse or the charisma of a pr- no, let me not blaspheme by saying it. But you understand that it is, as I believe spacers say, 'a long shot'."

He thinks for a moment, a dark look threatening to grow.

"I pray it is so. But these are heretics, poisoned by a false Word and a dark Path. It would require some exceptional ruse or the charisma of a pr- no, let me not blaspheme by saying it. But you understand that it is, as I believe spacers say, 'a long shot'."

He contemplates the tactical display for a moment. "As well, there is something to be said for the element of surprise."

"You walk the Path as one of the faithful. Appeal to common ground - this scheme would tear the Faith apart. Will all those of the Church simply turn to the Path at a push? Will other factions sit idly by as sectarian war rages, or will they seek advantage?"

Bornanew lets out a breath.

"You must not," he looks worried. "Setting aside... objections of the heart, complete destruction would almost certainly destroy the evidence required to identify the agent of the Path who has infiltrated the Cathedral of Holy Exodus."
options:
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastA:"You've given me a lot to think about."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastB:"You suggest that leniency will prompt cooperation? From Pathers?"
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastCa:"Archcurate Jaspis seems less forgiving of Pathers than you."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastC:"If I do not prove absolute domination of my enemy, they will not fear me."

text:
Bornanew takes a long breath. His eyes are dark, reflecting red pinpoints from the display.

"Doubtless such destruction would send an uncompromising message to these heretics who attempt to sew schism in the fields of the Mother Church. But." He raises his hand as if to grasp at his argument. "Total obliteration poses a serious risk of destroying the evidence needed to track down the agent of the Path who has infiltrated the Cathedral of Holy Exodus. We need those data stores."

He pauses, then turns to you. "Find the data first, through a marine raid, or some subterfuge. Then destroy the station. All would be served justice thus."
options:
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastA:"I shall consider this."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastBBlen:"I expected more talk of leniency from you, $global.jethroName."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastBBjasp:"I believe that Archcurate Jaspis would approve of this 'message'."

text:
"Did you?" he asks.

"You have made virtue of using a swift and strong hand in such matters. A Knight must learn to harden his heart when the situation calls for such... efficacy."

"Indeed." He casts his gaze up and far away. "I thought her harsh, before. Cynicism of age. But I see now with the eyes of a man."

"And I, for my part, made the choice to trust you." He looks to you.

"So, captain, the path is yours. I shall follow." He clasps his hands together, bowing his head slightly.
He raises an eyebrow at your noncommittal response, but nods acceptance, hands clasped.
"I do," he says. "They are merely human, as are we all. Despite a history written in blood, I do not find that our species thrives on hatred."
options:
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastA:"You've given me a lot to think about."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastBa:"I shall do as you ask - so far as is possible."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastCc:"If they force me to, I will do what is necessary."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastCa:"Archcurate Jaspis seems less forgiving of Pathers than you."

text: He nods acceptance, thankful, with hands clasped.

"Perhaps. Though alongside their fear, you will earn their hâte, and confirm in their minds all they say of you," he says. "You may well leave eternal enemies in your wake."

"Or corpses, I suppose," he adds grimly.
options:
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastA:"You've given me a lot to think about."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastCa:"Archcurate Jaspis seems less forgiving of Pathers than you."
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastCb:"I'll just win the battle without hurting anyone, is that what you want?"
bffi_psaConHubRespBlastCc:"If they force me to, I will do what is necessary."

text:
"Indeed." He casts his gaze up and far away. "Let not the sins of our mothers..." he says quietly, then looks back to you.

"Take my words not as judgment, but as a personal... a prayer, I suppose. Whâtever her words, whâtever her actions, she did choose to entrust her support to me in this, my task. And I, for my part, chose to trust you."

"So, captain, the path is yours." He drops his gaze and clasps his hands together.

He raises a skeptical eyebrow at you, but nods, hands clasped.

"I'd like nothing more, captain. Truly."

"In truth, it is not," he says. "I shan't ask the impossible." 

He clasps his hands together. "I trust you will achieve what you must, how you must."
He raises a skeptical eyebrow at you, but nods, hands clasped.
Bornanew lowers his head for just a moment, mumbling a short prayer to himself, then leaves the bridge to you and your officers.

id: # BFFI - INVESTIGATE_PATHER_STATION intro consult in medbay

text: Bornanew is conscious, you are told. You chief medical officer warns you against unnecessary stress; the healing process is fragile.
options:
bffi_stationMedConsult0:Consult with $global.jethroName
bffi_psApproachYourself:Handle this one yourself

text:
You decide to let Bornanew rest. You've got this.

All you need to do is beg, trick, or force the Pathers to reveal the source of their scan of the original glove. That glove has somehow remained, presumably untouched, in the vault of the Holy Cathedral of Exodus back on Tartessus this whole time.

Pathers are not known for keeping good digital records, but to nanoforge a copy of the glove, they would have had to. There may be metadata still in the computer systems connected to the nanoforge.

Or someone who simply knows the answers which you seek.

The attending medtech gives you a stern look as you step into the medbay despite the fact that you are the captain of the $shipOrFleet.

Bornanew's face is pale and bloated. His abdomen is covered in bandages and trailing tubes connected to a suite of murmuring autodocs.

"$playerFirstName," he says. "I appear to live." He takes a breath. "And to you I owe thanks. Captain, I mean. Apologies."

options: bffi_stationMedConBrave:"$global.jethroName. That was a brave thing you did on Chalcedon."
bffi_stationMedConStupid:"$global.jethroName. That was a stupid thing you did on Chalcedon."
bffi_stationMedConThank:"You should thank the medical team, they're the ones that saved you."
bffi_stationMedConWhatDo1:"We're in the $bffi_patherStationSystem. There's a Pather base."

text:
It looks like he tries to shrug, and fails.

"It was... it was..." he trails off, and closes his eyes. "I was terrified, captain. A coward. And yet, what else could be done?"

He opens his eyes. "It may be that is enough for a Knight." He shifts slightly, under the bandages and tubes. "The machines, drugs. They take the pain away. It still hurts."
"I have," he says. "And shall again, 'til they tire of it."

"Ah. Then Ulmus told us true." A flicker of a smile crosses Bornanew's face.

"We must... destroy the nanoforge. And find out who the mole is, in the cathedral. Tartessus." He closes his eyes and takes a slow breath.
options:
bffi_stationMedConIntendA:"I intend to negotiate with the Pather in charge here."
bffi_stationMedConIntendB:"I intend to send a marine raid. Seize datastores, destroy the forge."
bffi_stationMedConIntendC:"I intend to attack directly. Destroy the station."

text:
"They are... as human and fallible as any of us. To convince them, would be a miracle." He looks at you skeptically.

"If... that fails. Acquire hard data. The scan of the original glove, the metadata may tell us. Comms buffers. Archives. All useful." He looks at you, "I have a list of suspects. A mole in the Holy Cathedral of Exodus. Need evidence. Who to... put to inquisition." 

Defensive, he adds, "The rumors... slander. Mostly." His eyes are closed, his breathing shallow. "And... destroy the forge. Must be destroyed."

"The Pathers' reputation... they are as human as any. Not all heretical zealots. Fallible, fearful." He looks at you skeptically.

"I have a list of suspects. A mole in the Holy Cathedral of Exodus. Need evidence. Who to... put to inquisition. Acquire hard data. The scan of the original glove, the metadata may tell us. Comms buffers. Archives. All useful." 

He looks at you. "Inquisition... the rumors. Slander. Mostly." His eyes are closed, his breathing shallow. "And... destroy the forge. Must be destroyed."

"No!" He speaks a little too forcefully; a blinking light turns yellow.

"Risky... need acquire hard data. Scan of the original glove, the metadata. Comms buffers. Archives." He takes a deep breath then looks at you, "I have a list of suspects. A mole in the Holy Cathedral of Exodus. Need evidence. Who to... put to inquisition." 

He pauses, then says, "Inquisitors. The rumors... slander. Mostly." His eyes are closed, his breathing shallow. "And... destroy the forge. Must be destroyed."
"$PlayerSirOrMadam." Your chief medical officer appears behind you. "The patient needs rest. I must insist."
options:
bffi_stationMedConOutro2a:"Of course."
bffi_stationMedConOutro2b:"I will decide when he needs rest, doctor."

text:
Your med chief's face is crossed by a network of fine wrinkles, evidence of many cycles of service. They say nothing, but turn their head at a small and precise angle.

Bornanew's breathing is slow, regular. His eyes closed. Unconscious.

The expression on your med chief's face doesn't change, but the network of fine wrinkles tells of sufficient cycles of service such that they will stand firm against your overstep of authority.

"Nonetheless," your chief medical officer says, turning their head a small and precise angle.

Bornanew's breathing is slow, regular. His eyes closed. Unconscious.
You leave Bornanew to the stern care of your chief medical officer.

text:
The Pather's face twists with anger.  "Then burn with the rest, Molochian!" $heOrShe says, then disappears in a haze of compression artifacts.

The link plays a crackling buzz of a suppressed electronic warfare signal for a moment before your comms officer, considerately, shuts it off for you.

"Question me not!" True anger flashes across $hisOrHer eyes. "What know you of verity? You are blind to God, blind to the Word, you worship only credits and power. This I see full well!" 

"Would you have me submit to the creeping decay of the Church's heresy? Submit to the Knight-Inquisitors that I might be tortured into false confession?"

$HeOrShe pauses, breathing heavily for a moment, then more quietly says, "I was not made for such skulduggery. It is a Mammonite's trade." The words come more softly now. "Will you now fight or no? Better to die a martyr than a hypocrite."

"So you accuse me of sin, Molochian?" Anger flashes across $hisOrHer eyes. "What choice have I? Submit to the creeping decay of the Church's heresy? Submit to the Knight-Inquisitors that I be tortured into false confession?"

"Question not my piety, nor devotion. Give me arms, or none, and I shall face you or your brutes of the so-called Knights of Ludd to the end of my blood!"

$HeOrShe pauses a moment, almost overcome, to breathe heavily.

"I was not made for such skulduggery." The words come softly now. "It is a Mammonite's trade. But what choice is given?" $HeOrShe glares at you. "Will you make me a martyr now, or no?"

True anger flashes through  $hisOrHer eyes. "What choice have I? Shall I submit to the creeping decay of the Church's heresy? Submit to the Knight-Inquisitors that I might be tortured into false confession? It is they who slowly tear apart the Faith, not I! It is they who scorn the Word, not I!"

"Question not my piety, nor my devotion! Give me arms, or none, and I shall face you and your brutes of the so-called Knights of Ludd to the end of my blood!" $HeOrShe pauses a moment, almost overcome, to breathe heavily. "I was not made for such skulduggery." The words come more softly now. "It is a Mammonite's trade. But what choice is given? Will you make me a martyr now, or no?"

"So you have," $heOrShe admits. "I had thought that not possible - and assured it was not by Brother Sedge."

The Pather looks away. "That man would have told me anything to get his way, I think. And if somehow I didn't agree... I give thanks that it did not come to that."

"We're all given our place," $heOrShe says, both annoyed and discouraged. "The Path is not easy, nor does it provide comfort. For that, we were given the Word."

$HisOrHer eyes are searching, $hisOrHer devotion to this aspect of the cause obviously wavering. "Sedge was strong, and many followed him. Besides, he was backed by-" $HeOrShe catches $himOrHerself. "Backed by a better Walker by far, blessed be his Path."

"Well, it's done now, isn't it? That scheme is dead, and you bring here the technology of death."

$HisOrHer eyes narrow. "It doesn't matter now; I hear he was murdered, shot in the back by some spacer mercenary who is more machine than flesh."

"Merciful Providence forgive me," $hisOrHer tone softens slightly, "for to speak ill of the dead, and a martyr to our cause at that, but. The man was a beast and I am better pleased to find him on the other side of the infinite."
options:
bffi_stationTalk2sedgeA:"Then praise the Path! You're free of the beast and his schemes."
bffi_stationTalk2sedgeB:"It is I who shot Sedge. Though I'm less machine than you'd hope."

text:
"Mind, do hide your forked tongue." The Pather has a mean smile. "I see your obvious ploy, to turn me from the Path. But as is said by teachers wiser than I, 'the devil will lie by the truth itself'." $HeOrShe makes a show of looking you over through the comms.

"Nonetheless. I do find myself free of his... customs of leadership."

"Hah!" The Pather has a mean smile. "An obvious boast, typical of a Molochian." $HeOrShe makes a show of looking at you through the comms, "You couldn't have taken Sedge. He was a greater warrior than you or I, by far."

"Nonetheless, I weep not to be free of his... leadership."

"Hah!" The Pather has a mean smile. "An obvious boast, typical of a Molochian." $HeOrShe makes a show of looking at you through the comms, "You couldn't have taken Sedge... except by cowardly ambush, or some other deception. He was a greater warrior than you or I, by far."

"Nonetheless, I weep not to be free of his... leadership."

You explain that no devotion or zealotry, however righteous, can overcome the power of your $shipOrFleet, or the others that would follow if you failed to win the day.

Is $heOrShe so loyal to Sedge's plan that $heOrShe is willing to die for it - even after it's fallen apart? Besides, a schism across the whole of the ecumene of the Luddic faith would lead to a sectarian war which could see millions of Faithful dead. And not even for the cause of the Path, but for a falsehood!

$HeOrShe has a choice, you explain. Call it a Molochian bargain. Either this is a major loss of personnel, materiel, and the station, or it can be a minor loss of certain assets - for a failed barbaric scheme $heOrShe doesn't even really believe in.

You explain that there's one way that this is going to go, no devotion or zealotry can overcome the truth of the power of your $shipOrFleet - all ends result in $hisOrHer annihilation, and that of $hisOrHer followers, and of the station.

$HeOrShe has a choice, you explain. Call it a Molochian bargain, if it makes them feel better. Either this is a major loss of personnel, materiel, and the station, or it can be a minor loss of certain assets for a scheme $heOrShe doesn't even really believe in.

Is $heOrShe so loyal to Sedge's sect that $heOrShe is willing to die for it - even after it's already falling apart?
"Well," Bornanew says from beside you. "Let it not be said that you did not attempt diplomacy."

text: He nods, awaiting your next decision.

You explain that you will require comms buffers and datastores pertaining to Sedge's scheme. You will need the datastores of the nanoforge, and must be given access in order to destroy it.

No one need be hurt; it can be done quickly, without bloodshed, and without compromising $hisOrHer leadership status if $heOrShe secretly gives you their primary security codes. Blame it on the injection of a semi-sentient computer virus, a 'daemon' of the sort Molochians conjure in their AI-labs.

text: "Why should I trust you to keep your word?" $HeOrShe is clearly unhappy with your terms. "You're no servant of Providence following the holy Word passed to us by Ludd. How can there be light in your heart?"
$HeOrShe looks upon you, face now more open than before, as if almost convinced...

"Cease, I beg you." $HeOrShe puts $hisOrHer head in $hisOrHer hands. "I pray this is the correct Path, that you are not the monster you appear."

"I must accept your terms," $heOrShe says, "I shall pray for forgiveness. And may any hellfire of this world or the next fall upon myself first and not those I seek to protect."

text:
You convene your officers, and Bornanew, to evaluate the situation.

Pather resistance is expected to be fierce; the core of their militants have a zealous faith, little fear of death, and are often experienced fighters. However they eschew top of the line technology and combat enhancement, whether cybernetic or biochemical.

Bornanew clears his throat and speaks. "Certain classes of combat drugs are accepted among Luddic Path militants. They favor, ah, 'natural' sources." He takes in the confused looks. "Plants," he adds.

Capturing high-value datastores before they're wiped creates exceptional difficulties. The assault must be lightning-quick, and disabling rather than destructive as much as possible.

text:
You convene your officers to evaluate the situation.

Pather resistance is expected to be fierce; the core of their militants have a zealous faith, little fear of death, and are often experienced fighters. However they eschew top of the line technology and combat enhancement, whether cybernetic or biochemical.

Capturing high-value datastores before they're wiped creates exceptional difficulties. The assault must be lightning-quick, and disabling rather than destructive so much as possible.

You watch from the command center as marine squads launch the opening salvo from seemingly innocuous cargo containers. Powered armor units burst forth like locusts; combat techs inject malware into the station network, demolitions breach emergency blast doors, and assault teams cut a swath to key targets.

Nodes on the adaptive objective graph, woven through the station tactical display, are achieved one after another. Points blink from blood-red to amber or green, depending on found conditions. Indeed, some comms relays and data clusters auto-destruct upon unauthorized access, but others quietly fail, allowing techs to seize the contents.

The last red-indicated volume is the industrial section housing what is indeed the station's nanoforge; the Pathers had regrouped for a last stand. And so it is.

text:
They were caught off-guard. Despite the large numbers of armed zealots of the Path, your strike teams concentrated force where their defenses, fighting to the death, could not hold.

Teams return to waiting evac drones and shuttles, trailed by tethered spoils: memory blocks torn from their cradles, a few bagged prisoners.

A blue-white flash illuminates the returning victors. Behind them, the industrial section spins itself apart, structural plates shattered into glowing shards, struts tortuously bent, and that atmo which hasn't burned off in a glowing plume forms a rapidly expanding, dissipating cloud, illuminated from within by dying light.

The prize, turned over to your Tech officer's analysis team by a crew of sweating salvors, is a largely uncorrupted backup archive. Standard Domain tech, it hardly looks like the Pathers even knew it was quietly doing its job behind a thin access panel.

The technicians descend upon it with a suite of interfacers, passing every scrap of data to the analysts working in an isolated computing environment to reconstruct the system, starting with comms buffers and metadata.

A cloud of icons, sigils, words forms. Names, front companies, ships, codewords, and references to theological agitprop. Bornanew follows closely, his hands not nearly as deft with the interface as your dedicated techs.

You can see in his body language the exact moment that he finds something he recognizes.

"Keepfaith," Bornanew says. Fumbling with his datapad, the shared workspace highlights all references to that string of letters, then focuses, arranging comms logs and metadata into blocks, compressing everything else down into unobtrusive corners.

He leans back, his eyes darting around some internal catalogue. His paper notebook appears in his hands and he flips to a page as if to check something.

"Cedra Keepfaith," he explains. "A subcurate of the Holy Cathedral of Exodus on Tartessus, in the Eos Exodus system. She has authority over the scriptorium."

He sees your look, then explains. "Books. They repair and reproduce books for the library. And," his voice drops, "the vaults. She would have access."
options: bffi_psDestroyed3:"So this Keepfaith is your Pather mole?"

text:
"She would fit," he says, thinking. "My impression was that her ambitions in the Church have not been satisfied for many years, be that fair or no. She was not humble upon or meeting." 

"These logs appear to reference correspondence with her. She turned down credits, it appears. Would gratification of spite be payment enough?" 

He turns to you, done with speculation. "Certainly she did not act alone, but this connects her to the act. She must be arrested and taken to the Inquisitors for investigation and judgment."

A junior officer pokes their head into the briefing room, "$PlayerSirOrMadam, my apologies. The chief thought you'd want to see this."

Two burly salvors, still in their sweaty suit-liners, haul a crate and set it on the deckplates with a solid thump. With an impish grin, one of them looses the catches and together they pull the top-cover away.

It's filled with work-gloves. All of like chirality.

Bornanew lets out a breath.

"I ought to have anticipated this." He sounds annoyed with himself. "Costs no more to run a batch of one than of..." He reaches in, picking up a glove, then pressing into the cargo container to feel for density. "Five-twelve?" He turns to you. "Perhaps five hundred eleven, without our other hard-won specimen?"

He wipes his hands, as if something unpleasant had got on them. "These will need to be destroyed."

The last step is the capture of Bornanew's mole on Tartessus.

A cloud of icons, sigils, words forms. Names, front companies, ships, codewords, and references to theological agitprop. Your tech officer's hands blur as they manipulate an interface invisible to you; his subtle cybermods blink rapidly. And then stop, steady.

Their eyes refocus on the real world again, taking a moment to adapt to mundanity. "Something for your, ah, consultant. The Knight."

"At least according to the 'Ineffablist' school backed by the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis, among others. Here, though a minority of the Church ecumene, there is a local majority of so-called 'Mundanians'. Do not misunderstand me; they are not schismatics." He lets a moment of silence pass.

text:
You enter the great nave of the Cathedral of Holy Exodus.

Among towering columns are affixed recovered landing pods of the early Luddic refugee ships, now integrated into the vaults, painted with gilt and green, bedecked with candles and hanging glass ornament which break the beams of the sun - filtered through ranks of high windows - into thousands of rainbow shards endlessly refracting.

It is an ecstasy of light, a galaxy unto itself.

Bornanew pauses beside you, catching his breath, looking upward. Could one old work-glove and a lie tip all of this down to shatter upon the ground?

He leads you to a small alcove, invisible at the foot of the luminous architectural forest of cut stone and carved wood. And there, after exchanging words with an attendant, a door is revealed in the ornately carved wall.

You follow Bornanew's lead, stepping out of the glory of light into a cool, dark space. Your eyes take a moment to adjust to the dimness and relatively mundane - though still ornately hand-wrought - decor.

There are halls and offices here, lit not by candles but humble glowplates belched legion from Asher's nanoforge. The walls of cut stone are trimmed with alternating panels and mouldings of wood.

Bornanew's words and the token, presumably provided by the Archcurate Jaspis, clear a path through frowning oblate-secretaries and young Knight-novices on guard duty.

Not all are so dour; one or two initiates, scurrying by with armloads of books, greet Bornanew by name.

You follow Bornanew into a long room, a gallery of columns intermingled with bookcases; cleverly designed lightwells - and, surely, a series of mirrors - provide shafts of sunlight, motes dancing with little concern in their small world within this analog archive. 

Heads turn to follow your progress past desks stacked with paper books, writing implements, and even the occasional low-fidelity interface terminal.

Your tactical sense notes the figures at side-doors who disappear purposefully, clearly headed off to spread this news and summon gawkers - or allies.

Your weapon-hand twitches.

text:
Bornanew approaches a proud figure all in holy vestments at the head of the room.

"Subcurate Cedra Keepfaith." $global.jethroName's voice is confident.

The figure turns, finally. "Novice," she replies, tone intent on the gradient of authority. "Why have you returned to my scriptorium? Have you been assigned additional penitential duties?"

A mocking smile flits upon her lip.

"It was you," Bornanew says with perfect earnestness.

"You scanned the glove said to be of Ludd. You did this in the vault of this cathedral, and you knowingly passed that scan data to an agent of the heretics called the Luddic Path. Their aim, and yours, was to sow discord within the Church of Galactic Redemption. This I shall prove before the judgement of the Inquisitors."

"For these crimes," he raises his voice against the growing murmurs, "against the Holy Church, by the authority of the Reverend Archcurate Sophronia Jaspis, as a Knight of Ludd, I do now place you under arrest."

"It was you," Bornanew says with perfect earnestness. "Traitor."

"You scanned the glove said to be of Ludd. You did this in the vault of this cathedral, and you knowingly passed that scan data to an agent of the heretics called the Luddic Path. Their aim, and yours, was to sow discord within the Church of Galactic Redemption. This I know, and this truth the Inquisitors shall pull from you!"

"For these crimes," he raises his voice against the growing murmurs, "against the Holy Church, by the authority of the Reverend Archcurate Sophronia Jaspis, as a Knight of Ludd, I do now place you under arrest."

There are gasps, and a muffled clatter as some initiate drops their work. Keepfaith's gaze, already chilly, turns poisonous upon Bornanew.

"Blind Jaspis has no power here," she says. "And you, a mere novice of a Knight, making ungodly accusations against a superior? I shall assign you further penance if a full apology is not made... instantly!"

"Brother Bornanew," a Knight-Captain appears behind the angry subcurate.

He attempts a comradely tone, his carbine slung at his side.

"Please," he says. "Let us go together to resolve this misunderstanding with the archcurate of this cathedral. There is no need to herein bear the hammer and the sword-"

"There is no misunderstanding, brother," Bornanew cuts off the Knight-Captain's words, keeping his eyes on Keepfaith. "I bear my quest not lightly, and seek to fulfill my duty sworn to the Excubitor Orbis."

He reaches into his robes.

"Knight-Captain," says Keepfaith, drawing her breath. "Arrest your novice! He's to be punished for irreverence toward the authority of the Church and, and for these wild accusations!"

Bornanew steps forward, prompting the Knight-Captain to raise the carbine he had held at his side. A hint of victory settles into Keepfaith's eyes.

"You dare assault a curate in this holy place?" she says, projecting her voice to all corners of the room, ringing harshly. "Is there no low to which this too-old novice won't sink?"
notes: # intentional use of "curate" instead of subcurate btw -dgb

text:
Bornanew draws his hand from his robes. You see dangling the token you glimpsed only briefly before: carved in wood, on a string, is a flower growing out of an oddly shaped shoe. A token of Jaspis?

"Beg hear me, Faithful!" Bornanew's head turns; he meets the eyes of those assembled, from the apprentices of the scriptorium to the Knight-Captain and, finally, the subcurate before him.

"Cedra Keepfaith invited the serpent of heresy into this holy place that it might spread a venom in the heart of the Church. Already it has poisoned one among us."
notes: # cut to move scene along faster

text:
He steps forward again, pushing the barrel of the Knight-captain's carbine into his chest. The Knight-Captain looks desperately uncomfortable. "Brother," he says again, pleading.

"Do your duty, Knight-Captain!" shouts Keepfaith. 

Bornanew takes another step, bodily pushing back the Knight-Captain, still cradling the carbine. Finger on the trigger.

text:
A pathetic struggle ensues, Keepfaith's hands locked around the Knight-Captain's, who struggles to keep the weapon under his control.

Bornanew simply watches until, finally, both Curate and Knight-captain tumble into a workdesk, sending papers and tools flying.

Expressions among the apprentices range from appalled second-hand embarrassment to impish delight.

Red-faced, the Knight-Captain, regains his feet with Subcurate Keepfaith restrained in his arms. There is little dignity remaining for either.

Bornanew, meanwhile, remains standing in a stray beam of sunlight - a product of the clever arrangement of lightwells. The effect places him as the glowing centerpiece to an instructive tableau.

This can't have been happenstance; Bornanew's insistence on pushing forward, toward the light...

Coincidence or performance, the invisible consensus is irrevocably turned against Keepfaith.

"It was under control!" she screams. "It would never have been necessary if you Knights attended your duties. You're lax, lax and blind!"

"Come now, sister," says the Knight-Captain, leading her away. "Be not careless with your words, for the Inquisitors shall hear all..."

text:
The Knight-Captain's eyes go wide, taking in your action.

A shot erupts from the carbine and you are anointed by a spray of blood.

Horrified, the Knight-Captain shoves Subcurate Keepfaith aside and safeties his weapon. She crashes into a workdesk, papers and tools and dignity scattered all around.

There are screams. Is there a doctor? Orders are shouted. The Knight-Captain is tearing his robe, holding a piece of cloth to Bornanew's wound. The red bubbles up into the white.

Ignoring the chaos surrounding, you rush to Bornanew's aid, assisting the Knight-Captain in attempting to staunch the flow of blood.

The Knight-Captain appears trained for this, working with grim determination, directing you without hesitation as needed.

Pulling your eyes away from the bloody scene, you see Subcurate Keepfaith realizing that the chaos she has caused presents her with the perfect opportunity to slip away.

It takes just two long strides and you catch her arm. Bornanew's blood is slick on your hand, and she pulls, hard, screaming admonitions but does not manage escape.

It is a close thing, that line between life and death. Like the thin line of atmo when looking down upon a breathing planet; nothing more than a razor's edge from the deathly black.

You notice the Knight-Captain's arms are red up to his elbows. And yours, too, as you assist. 

There are shouts, and running footsteps. A doctor arrives, with assistent, who shoves you and the Knight-Captain aside after only the briefest exchange.

You stand back, dripping. The Knight-Captain is shaken, and, with a despairing look, fully removes the magazine of his carbine.

The bloody scene is illuminated by a ray of sunlight, a violent tableau. Shocked apprentices are recruited to help carry Bornanew elsewhere. Some kind of on-site infirmary.

You and the Knight-Captain turn toward one another to exchange a look. His eyes are wide.

Subcurate Keepfaith has disappeared.

The rogue subcurate's shouts are quelled by the horrified faces of her apprentices.

"I had it under control!" she says, not quite pleading. "It would never have been necessary if the Knights attended their duties. They're lax, lax and blind!"

Shouts, and running steps. A doctor arrives to take over from the Knight-Captain, who, shaken, stands back from the bloody scene illuminated in a ray of sunlight. Shocked apprentices are recruited to help carry Bornanew elsewhere; some kind of on-site infirmary.

The Knight-Captain spots you, finally, and approaches. His arms are red to the elbows, his robes turn and spattered with the blood of his brother Knight.

"Come now, sister," he says coldly. "Be not careless with your words, for the Inquisitors shall hear all of it. All of it."

text:
You know it is over when the Knight-Captain goes quiet and still. His forearms are red with blood. Everywhere, blood, alit by the shaft of sunlight. Pale faces of apprentices look on, horrified.

Bornanew's body, weakened by Sedge's brutal attack, simply cannot hold out. In a matter of seconds, his life is spilled out of him onto the floor of the scriptorium.

"I had it under control," Keepfaith says, not quite pleading now. "It would never have been necessary if the Knights attended their duties. They're lax, lax and blind!"

No one is listening.

text:
You shove into Bornanew, your speed making up for his mass, tumbling him away from the barrel of the gun. 

A shot erupts from the carbine.

Later, you watch a recording of the incident and see your blood anoint an arc of junior assistants of the scriptoria. A fine rain of droplets stains their papers and books.

In the moment, however, the world has become indistinct. A feeling like the familiar drift of zero-g overcomes you.

You fall back, ears buzzing, your eyes brought into line with the mural on the ceiling. Painted clouds and stars dance above you, attached to interlocking toothed wheels. The turning spheres of creation, the orbits and movements of celestial mechanics given literal form.

It's funny to you, the spacer, but you cannot laugh.

Your body is not responding to your commands. Your ship is adrift. Fluxed out. Vent.

Darkness.

* * *

The cadre of Luddic Knights guarding the Cathedral of Holy Exodus defer to Bornanew's authority.

Keepfaith is delivered to your shuttle, restrained, and now - away from her small kingdom - is timid but glowering.

"I will be absolved," she says. "You shall witness my righteousness before the end."

Bornanew shakes his head but does not reply. One of your guards switches on the dampening field so that you will not be troubled by Keepfaith's words.
You return to your $shipOrFleet. After the rogue subcurate is placed in the brig you meet with Bornanew in a briefing room.

"We must take her to Hesperus," he says. "To the Knight Inquisitors and their justice."

He stares out at a display of starry space, subdued.

"I shall submit my evidence, then the Inquisitors will question her before a quorum of authority determined by the severity of her crime. I suspect more than one archcurate shall be required."
options:
bffi_arrestOutroAskPenance:"Are you not pleased? Won't your penance be complete?"
bffi_arrestCurateEnd:Leave him to his thoughts

text:
He turns to you.

"Pleased? That is difficult. What we have uncovered is deeply concerning. As for my penance," he grimaces. "You have not met the Excubitor Orbis of Hesperus."

He turns back to the stars.

He turns to you.

"Pleased? That is difficult. What we have uncovered is deeply concerning. As for my penance," he grimaces. "The Excubitor Orbis of Hesperus would have me punished for the sins he imagines of the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis, and others who do not fully agree with him besides, I am sure. There will be little wordly relief within his domain. But to there I must return."

He turns back to the stars.

He turns to you.

"Pleased? That is difficult. What we have uncovered is deeply concerning. As for my penance," he grimaces. "You have met Excubitor Orbis Oak, yes?"

bffi_arrestOutroPenUnderstand:"Yes. I understand your concern."
bffi_arrestOutroPenForgive:"Maybe he'll be in a forgiving mood after you deliver a traitor and the false relic."

text: He nods, then turns back to the stars.

text: You dream of stars, but they flit and dance in the tapestry of the night to music you can't quite hear.

* * *

You awake to the familiar hum of your flagship, in orbit.

For a moment, your mind drifts without context. A machine chirps helpfully somewhere.

Your vision is a blur, slowly resolving like a bad comm-link signal running poorly-adjusted inference algorithms.

There's a human-shaped blob. The details come rushing back.

text:
The shape leans forward and turns into Bornanew.

"Yes, captain," he says. "And you are alive, thank Providence. And your medical team, of course."

At least, that's what you try to say. What leaves your mouth is an indeterminate mush of phonemes.

The shape leans forward and turns into Bornanew.

"You've been shot," he says. "Try not to move."

At least, that's what you try to say. What leaves your mouth is an indetermine mush of phonemes.

The shape leans forward and turns into Bornanew.

"Good morning, captain," he says. "By local reference, anyway."

The shape leans forward and turns into Bornanew.

"It's just me. Jethro," he says. "Good morning captain. By ah, local reference, anyway."

The shape leans forward and turns into Bornanew.

"It's just me. Jeth... um, Jeff," he says. "Good morning captain. By ah, local reference, anyway."

"Knight-Captain Larix applied first aid to you." Bornanew speaks softly. "I do think he saved your life." 

He leans back. "And I, I managed to catch Keepfaith before she could flee. She's in the brig, now. And no longer a subcurate. The Reverend Archcurate of the Cathedral of Holy Exodus - Bywater, his name is - was quite distraught about the shooting. He revoked her curateship."

He pauses a moment to let everything sink in.

"Please, not yet," he says. "Be at ease."

"Keepfaith should be given over to the Knight-Inquisitors, on Hesperus. She still must be investigated for her crimes and put before a quorum of appropriate authority for judgement." He pauses. "Her other crimes, I mean. We have a score of witnesses for the shooting, and..."

He trails off, knowing little need be said of that just now. Another voice speaks, from outside the room - the medical recovery bay.

Bornanew nods at some instruction then, standing up, looks back to you.

"Thank you, captain. For my life, again."

You are of little use in the search, being unfamiliar with the cathedral. The Knight-Captain whose name you almost forgot to ask - "Larix," he said, "Knight-Captain Dejan Larix" before shouting orders to a hastily assembled squad of Knight-Novices, all obviously far younger than Bornanew.

Bornanew, for one, is stabilized in the infirmary, part of the sprawling church infrastructure spilling from the cathedral.

* * *

You are nearly forgotten in the chaos. Before long, a meek apprentice directs you to the offices of the Luddic Knights where you make a statement and discuss the incident with the Knight-Captain. You only just remember to ask him his name. 

"Larix," he says, "Knight-Captain Dejan Larix" before shouting orders to a hastily assembled squad of Knight-novices, all obviously far younger than Bornanew.

Who, for one, is stabilized in the infirmary, part of the sprawling church infrastructure spilling from the cathedral.

The resident physician is more practical than traditional, and sees fit to consult with your own chief medical officer. A patient transfer is arranged, and even a special dispensation (granted by the embarrassed archcurate) to land a shuttle on a field near to the Cathedral of Holy Exodus.

The implicit use of technologies which would be awkward to employ on Church grounds to save Bornanew's life is left unsaid.

The cadre of Luddic Knights guarding the Cathedral of Holy Exodus defer to Bornanew's authority, despite his unconsciousness.

Keepfaith, just Cedra now that her subcurateship has been summarily revoked by order of the archcurate, is delivered to you flagship. She is restrained, and now - away from her small kingdom - timid, though still glowering.

"I will be absolved," she says hoarsely. "You shall witness my righteousness before the end."

One of your guards closes the brig door, its weight silent.

The cadre of Luddic Knights guarding the Cathedral of Holy Exodus defer to Bornanew's authority, despite his unconsciousness.

Keepfaith, just Cedra now that her subcurateship has been summarily revoked by order of the archcurate, is nowhere to be found. You can only speculate that she called upon her Pather contacts for assistance.

It is some time before Bornanew regains consciousness.

Your chief medical officer hovers nearby, giving you a stern look. You know you will not be allowed to overtrouble their patient.

"Inquisitors," he whispers, then carefully turns his head toward a display of starry space and the orb of Tartessus. "Justice."

Later, back on your flagship in orbit, you find instructions on Bornanew's cheap datapad.

"If you hear these words, then I am returned to the arms of Providence," it begins.

You are to take his mortal remains to Hesperus, to the Knights of Ludd, so that it may rest with the fellow-walkers he knew for only a short time.

* * *

You are nearly forgotten in the chaos. Before long, a meek apprentice directs you to the offices of the Luddic Knights where you make a statement and discuss the incident with the Knight-Captain. You only just remember to ask him his name. 

"Larix," he says, "Knight-Captain Dejan Larix" before shouting orders to a hastily assembled squad of Knight-novices, all obviously far younger than Bornanew is.

Than Bornanew was.

The Knight-Captain helped organize a search for Keepfaith.

You had almost forgotten to ask his name - "Larix," he said, drained of emotion. "Dejan Larix."

"For the small moment I knew him, I knew he was a good man. A good Knight. Damned but that it had to be my hand..." he trailed off then, hiding his eyes.

You left him to return to your shuttle.

Heavy doors open from the rockface inviting your landing party into the dimly lit hangar beyond.

A score of armored Knights march forth - like small, segmented dolls compared with the scale of the door, but as you approach you note their height. They must be the tallest farm-fed children of Gilead and Tartessus, all aclad in polished white armor, half-capes slack in the near-vacuum.

They turn precisely aside, weapons held vertical.

She interfered with Knight-Captain Larix's weapon and shot me."

Oak's eyes narrow. Oak's clerk looks between him and Bornanew curiously, but she also holds her tongue.

"The traitor fled and the Knights posted to Tartessus have not found her," Bornanew continues, criticism obvious in his tone. "The Reverend Archcurate Bywater, of the Cathedral of Holy Exodus, saw fit to strip Keepfaith of the station of Subcurate in absentia. Nonetheless, she has thus far escaped punishment. If you simply order the Inquisitors to-"

Gideon Oak raises a hand, almost a parody of the gesture frozen in the glove that started this all. Bornanew silences himself with obvious reluctance.

id: # BFFI deal with the glove now

text:
Bornanew bows, then places the sample container he was carrying on the hangar floor.

Without rush or flourish, he unlatches the cover and withdraws it to reveal the false relic, the upthrust work-glove which was never touched by Ludd.

Bornanew does not bow, but matches Oak's glare as he places the sample container onto the hangar floor.

Without rush or flourish, he unlatches the cover and withdraws it to reveal the false relic, the upthrust work-glove which was never touched by Ludd.

A pair of your bodyguards step forward, carrying the sample case between them.

They place the container on the hangar floor and without rush or flourish unlatch the cover and withdraw it to reveal the false relic, the upthrust work-glove which was never touched by Ludd.

Oak turns his head ever so slightly. "Destroy it," he rumbles.

The clerk mutters into her cuff.

Oak is content to remain silent as technicians of the Knights wheel out a heavy crucible of sorts, festooned with tubes and patterned heat-dispersal tiles. They halt it several meters from you, then scurry over to retrieve the glove - still in its open sample case - and place the whole lot carefully into the blackened interior. They slowly lower and then seal the heavy top-cover then adjust some knobs and switches. The lead tech looks to the Excubitor Orbis.

He gives a tiny nod.

A dull roar sounds from the device, like a torrential waterfall behind a hardened airlock. Indentations in the tiled carapace begin to glow; you can feel the radiated heat on your exposed skin.

The clerk shuffles nervously, flipping open a notebook, pausing a moment, then flipping it closed. She looks at the Excubitor Orbis.

He nods at her, then looks back to you.

The clerk clears her throat.

"Excubitor Orbis," her eyes dart at you and Bornanew. "$playerSirOrMadam. Novice."

"There is a matter of the Demarch of Olinadu. A Menes Yaribay? He claims he was promised payments for the, um, purchase of a relic."

You hadn't thought Oak could look any more displeased.

The clerk coughs, "We, ah," she flips a page on a paper notebook.

"While the Church does not recognize the purchase of,  um," her eyes flicker to the crucible, which has stopped glowing, but ticks away as it cools. "The object in question. However, due to the promise made by a... representative," she looks quickly at Bornanew, then away. "It was proposed that a... polite settlement with Mister Yaribay be made using the reward credits originally authorized by the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis..."

"Do it," says the Excubitor Orbis.

You can't quite see if Bornanew is blushing, because his head is bowed toward Oak.

The clerk coughs, "We, ah," she flips a page on a paper notebook.

"While the Church does not recognize the purchase of,  um," her eyes flicker to the crucible, which has stopped glowing, but ticks away as it cools. "The object in question. However, due to the promise made by a... representative," she looks quickly at Bornanew, then away. "It was proposed that a... polite settlement with Mister Yaribay be made using the reward credits originally authorized by the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis..."

"Do it," says the Excubitor Orbis.

The clerk clears her throat.

"Excubitor Orbis," her eyes dart at you and Bornanew. "$playerSirOrMadam. Novice."

"There is a matter of the Demarch of Olinadu. A Menes Yaribay? He claims damages from an... incident. He claims that a valuable object was stolen during a violent raid on his personal compound. And, um, there is a list of potential suspects..."

You hadn't thought Oak could look any more displeased.

The clerk coughs, "We, ah," she flips a page on a paper notebook.

"While the Church does not recognize the legitimacy of, um," her eyes flicker to the crucible, which has stopped glowing, but ticks away as it cools. "The object in question. It was proposed that a... polite settlement of affairs with Mister Yaribay be made using the reward credits originally authorized by the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis for..."

"Do it," says the Excubitor Orbis.

You can't quite see if Bornanew is blushing, because his head is bowed toward Oak.

The clerk coughs, "We, ah," she flips a page on a paper notebook.

"While the Church does not recognize the legitimacy of, um," her eyes flicker to the crucible, which has stopped glowing, but ticks away as it cools. "The object in question. It was proposed that a... polite settlement of affairs with Mister Yaribay be made using the reward credits originally authorized by the Reverend Archcurate Jaspis for..."

"Do it," says the Excubitor Orbis.

You can't quite see if Bornanew is blushing, because his head is bowed toward Oak.

options:
bffi_outro1a:"We've delivered the false relic and destroyed its source."
bffi_outro1b:"I believe this fulfills Novice Bornanew's 'quest' of penance."
bffi_outro1c:"$global.jethroName. We done here?"

text:
"I t-think that's everything," the clerk says, looking between you and Oak.

Excubitor Orbis Gideon Oak exhales, meeting your gaze. Then, without a word, he turns away and begins walking back to the far doorway casting its long wedge of light into the armored hangar.

After a respectful pause, the clerk and guards rush to follow.

Oak ignores you.

"The novice still wears a crown of pride," Oak says toward Bornanew. "Still he has much to repent."

Bornanew turns slightly aside, keeping his eyes toward the Excubitor Orbis. "I shall be staying here a while, I think," he says softly.

Oak ignores you.

"The novice still wears a crown of pride," Oak says toward Bornanew. "Still he has much to repent."

Bornanew's look hardens. He matches the remorseless gaze of the Excubitor Orbis with his own. "If it be so by Providence," he says.

"No," says Oak. "The novice still wears a crown of pride. He has much to repent."

Bornanew turns aside and says quietly to you, "I shall be staying here a while, I think."

"No," says Oak. "The novice still wears a crown of pride. He has much to repent."

Bornanew does not turn aside from Gideon Oak, matching his remorseless look with his own. "The Excubitor Orbis believes there is much sin for which I must pay. I shall be staying here, as it pleases him."

Oak, for his part, looks very much less than pleased at Bornanew's commentary. His clerk looks mortified.

"Yes," says Bornanew, still caught in a staring-contest with Oak. "You are. I shall be staying here a while, I think."

The world's smallest smile makes a new crease on the Excubitor Orbis' face as he turns to you.

"Novice Bornanew owes you much. But still he wears a crown of pride. Still he must repent."
